name: World in 2016
description: The world as it was in January 2016. The Ukrainian crisis, the Syrian civil war, the rise of the Islamic State and tensions in the South China Sea define a period of great geopolitical instability.

NATO.name: NATO
NATO.description: North Atlantic collective defense military alliance

EU.name: European Union
EU.description: European political and economic union (the United Kingdom is still a member in January 2016)

initial_events.title: Saudi Arabia severs ties with Iran
initial_events.description: Following the execution of Shia cleric Nimr al-Nimr and the attack on its embassy in Tehran, Saudi Arabia severed diplomatic relations with Iran on January 3, 2016.

initial_events.title: Ongoing Russo-Ukrainian conflict
initial_events.description: The Minsk II accords have not ended fighting in the Donbass. Russia has controlled Crimea since 2014 and intermittent fighting continues in eastern Ukraine.

initial_events.title: Syrian crisis and migration
initial_events.description: The Syrian civil war enters its 5th year. The Islamic State controls large swaths of territory. Europe faces an unprecedented migration crisis.

initial_events.title: Tensions in the South China Sea
initial_events.description: China continues militarizing artificial islands in the South China Sea, causing tensions with the Philippines, Vietnam and the United States.

initial_events.title: North Korean nuclear test
initial_events.description: North Korea announces it tested a hydrogen bomb on January 6, 2016, triggering international condemnation.

USA.name: United States of America
USA.description: La plus grande puissance économique et militaire mondiale, chef de file de l'ordre libéral occidental.
USA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — United States of America]
- Political landscape: President Barack Obama in his final year; divisive 2016 presidential primaries underway with Donald Trump and Hillary Clinton as frontrunners.
- Key challenges: Mass shootings debate, Affordable Care Act implementation, racial tensions following Ferguson and Baltimore protests, opioid epidemic escalating.
- Regional tensions: Leading coalition against ISIS in Iraq and Syria; managing fallout from 2015 JCPOA nuclear deal with Iran; pivot to Asia strategy.
- Economic situation: Steady recovery from 2008 crisis, unemployment at 5%, Fed raised interest rates in December 2015 for the first time in nearly a decade.
- Strategic assets: World's largest military budget (~$600B), 11 carrier strike groups, global network of ~800 overseas bases, nuclear triad, dominant tech sector.
- 2016 context: JCPOA Implementation Day on January 16 lifts Iran sanctions; TPP signed but not ratified; Flint water crisis gaining national attention.

CHN.name: People's Republic of China
CHN.description: Deuxième économie mondiale en pleine ascension, puissance régionale cherchant à étendre son influence mondiale.
CHN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — China]
- Political landscape: President Xi Jinping consolidating power, anti-corruption campaign intensifying; one-child policy officially ended January 1, 2016.
- Key challenges: Economic growth slowing to ~6.9% (lowest in 25 years), stock market turmoil with circuit breakers triggered in early January 2016, capital flight concerns.
- Regional tensions: Aggressive island-building in South China Sea, tensions with Japan over Senkaku/Diaoyu Islands, growing assertiveness toward Taiwan.
- Economic situation: World's second-largest economy transitioning from export-led to consumption-driven growth; yuan devaluation concerns roiling global markets.
- Strategic assets: 2.3 million active military personnel (world's largest), rapidly modernizing navy, growing nuclear arsenal, space program, massive foreign reserves (~$3.3T).
- 2016 context: One Belt One Road initiative expanding; AIIB launched in January 2016; first overseas military base being built in Djibouti; new aircraft carrier under construction.

RUS.name: Russian Federation
RUS.description: Ancienne superpuissance cherchant à restaurer son influence, engagée en Ukraine et en Syrie.
RUS.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Russia]
- Political landscape: President Vladimir Putin with high domestic approval; opposition marginalized; state media dominance; Nemtsov assassination in 2015 still reverberating.
- Key challenges: Western sanctions over Crimea annexation, oil price collapse devastating budget revenues, ruble devaluation, international isolation deepening.
- Regional tensions: Military intervention in Syria since September 2015 supporting Assad; ongoing hybrid war in eastern Ukraine (Donbass); frozen conflicts in Georgia and Moldova.
- Economic situation: GDP contracted ~3.7% in 2015, deep recession from combined sanctions and oil price crash; inflation above 12%; reserves declining.
- Strategic assets: World's largest nuclear arsenal (~7,300 warheads), modernizing military, permanent UNSC seat, vast energy resources, advanced air defense systems (S-400).
- 2016 context: Syria intervention reshaping Middle East dynamics; Turkey relations in crisis after November 2015 shoot-down of Russian jet; Minsk II ceasefire largely holding but unresolved.

DEU.name: Federal Republic of Germany
DEU.description: Locomotive économique de l'Europe et leader de l'UE, confrontée à la crise des réfugiés.
DEU.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Germany]
- Political landscape: Chancellor Angela Merkel (CDU) under intense pressure over refugee policy; 'Wir schaffen das' stance dividing the country; AfD gaining support.
- Key challenges: Over 1 million refugees arrived in 2015, integration and housing crisis; Cologne New Year's Eve mass assaults dominating headlines in January 2016.
- Regional tensions: Leading EU response to Ukraine crisis, key interlocutor with Russia; balancing solidarity within EU as eastern members resist refugee quotas.
- Economic situation: Europe's largest economy performing well with ~1.7% growth, low unemployment (~4.5%), budget surplus, strong exports; Volkswagen emissions scandal impact.
- Strategic assets: EU's economic engine, major NATO contributor, strong manufacturing and export base, diplomatic influence as EU de facto leader.
- 2016 context: Refugee crisis reshaping European politics; Merkel negotiating EU-Turkey deal; NSA surveillance scandal straining US-German relations; Deutsche Bank facing massive legal fines.

FRA.name: French Republic
FRA.description: Puissance nucléaire et membre permanent du Conseil de Sécurité, très active militairement en Afrique et au Moyen-Orient.
FRA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — France]
- Political landscape: President Francois Hollande deeply unpopular; state of emergency extended after November 2015 Bataclan/Paris attacks; Front National gaining ground.
- Key challenges: Terrorism threat at highest level; unemployment stubbornly above 10%; social divisions over secularism and Muslim integration; labor law reform planned.
- Regional tensions: Active military operations in Mali (Barkhane) and Central Africa; participating in anti-ISIS coalition; maintaining presence in francophone Africa.
- Economic situation: Sluggish growth (~1.3%), high public debt (~96% GDP), persistent budget deficits; struggling to meet EU fiscal rules.
- Strategic assets: Independent nuclear deterrent (~300 warheads), aircraft carrier Charles de Gaulle, permanent UNSC seat, significant overseas territories, strong defense industry.
- 2016 context: Post-attack security overhaul; COP21 Paris Agreement just adopted December 2015; labor law protests brewing; 2017 presidential campaign beginning to take shape.

GBR.name: United Kingdom
GBR.description: Puissance mondiale cherchant sa voie entre l'Europe et le monde anglophone. Le référendum Brexit est prévu en juin 2016.
GBR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — United Kingdom]
- Political landscape: PM David Cameron (Conservative majority); EU referendum promised for June 2016; Cameron negotiating reforms with Brussels; Labour under new leader Jeremy Corbyn.
- Key challenges: Brexit debate dominating politics; Scottish independence pressure after 2014 referendum; NHS funding crisis; austerity measures ongoing.
- Regional tensions: Active in anti-ISIS coalition; parliamentary vote authorized airstrikes in Syria December 2015; maintaining Falklands defense; Northern Ireland peace fragile.
- Economic situation: Growth around 2.2%, outperforming most EU peers; low unemployment; London as global financial center; significant current account deficit.
- Strategic assets: Nuclear deterrent (Trident), permanent UNSC seat, Five Eyes intelligence alliance, global financial center, special relationship with US, strong soft power.
- 2016 context: EU referendum campaign about to intensify; Trident renewal debate; immigration a top voter concern; House of Lords reform stalled; flooding in northern England.

JPN.name: Japan
JPN.description: Troisième économie mondiale, cherchant à se réarmer face aux menaces chinoises et nord-coréennes sous Abe.
JPN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Japan]
- Political landscape: PM Shinzo Abe (LDP) with strong parliamentary majority; pursuing 'Abenomics' economic revitalization and security normalization agenda.
- Key challenges: Aging population crisis (26% over 65), persistent deflationary pressures, public debt over 230% GDP, Fukushima decommissioning ongoing.
- Regional tensions: Territorial disputes with China (Senkaku) and South Korea (Dokdo/Takeshima); North Korean nuclear/missile threat; comfort women agreement with South Korea December 2015.
- Economic situation: Third-largest world economy with ~0.5% growth; Bank of Japan considering negative interest rates; TPP trade deal signed but unratified.
- Strategic assets: Technologically advanced Self-Defense Forces, US alliance with ~50,000 troops stationed, world-class navy, advanced missile defense, strong tech/manufacturing base.
- 2016 context: New security laws allowing limited collective self-defense effective; reinterpreting Article 9; hosting G7 summit in Ise-Shima in May 2016; Okinawa base relocation disputes.

IND.name: India
IND.description: Plus grande démocratie du monde, économie à croissance rapide naviguant entre grandes puissances.
IND.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — India]
- Political landscape: PM Narendra Modi (BJP) in second year; pursuing economic reforms and Hindu nationalist agenda; opposition Congress party weakened.
- Key challenges: Poverty and inequality, inadequate infrastructure, air pollution crisis, communal tensions, Naxalite-Maoist insurgency in rural areas.
- Regional tensions: Ongoing tensions with Pakistan over Kashmir; Chinese border disputes; growing concern over Chinese String of Pearls encirclement; influence competition in Indian Ocean.
- Economic situation: World's fastest-growing major economy (~7.5% growth), 'Make in India' initiative, FDI liberalization, but rural distress and banking NPAs a concern.
- Strategic assets: 1.3 million active military, nuclear arsenal (~120 warheads), space program with Mars orbiter, growing navy, large diaspora, IT sector powerhouse.
- 2016 context: Modi's diplomatic outreach including surprise visit to Pakistan in December 2015; Pathankot airbase attack January 2, 2016 straining India-Pakistan ties; GST reform pending.

BRA.name: Federative Republic of Brazil
BRA.description: Plus grande puissance d'Amérique du Sud, traversant une grave crise économique et politique (affaire Lava Jato).
BRA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Brazil]
- Political landscape: President Dilma Rousseff (PT) facing impeachment proceedings; massive Petrobras corruption scandal (Operation Car Wash) engulfing political class.
- Key challenges: Deep political crisis, Zika virus outbreak declared emergency, crumbling infrastructure, rising crime, environmental degradation in Amazon.
- Regional tensions: Regional leadership contested; Mercosur stagnating; Venezuela crisis creating diplomatic tensions; border security concerns.
- Economic situation: Severe recession with GDP contracting ~3.8%, inflation above 10%, unemployment rising rapidly, real depreciating, credit rating downgraded to junk.
- Strategic assets: Largest economy and military in Latin America, vast natural resources, pre-salt oil reserves, agricultural superpower, soft power through cultural exports.
- 2016 context: Rio Olympics in August 2016 amid infrastructure concerns; impeachment of Rousseff looming; Zika threatening Olympics; Lula under investigation; fiscal crisis deepening.

CAN.name: Canada
CAN.description: Démocratie libérale et partenaire privilégié des États-Unis, sous la direction du nouveau premier ministre Trudeau.
CAN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Canada]
- Political landscape: PM Justin Trudeau (Liberal) newly elected October 2015 with majority government; promising progressive agenda, reconciliation with Indigenous peoples, and climate action.
- Key challenges: Oil price collapse hitting Alberta hard, Indigenous reconciliation (Truth and Reconciliation Commission report 2015), housing costs in Vancouver/Toronto, refugee resettlement.
- Economic situation: GDP growth slowing to ~1% due to oil price crash; two-speed economy (struggling Alberta vs. growing Ontario/Quebec); Canadian dollar at 12-year low.
- Regional tensions: Close US ally (NATO, Five Eyes, NORAD); committed to resettling 25,000 Syrian refugees; withdrawing fighter jets from anti-ISIS combat mission.

AUS.name: Australia
AUS.description: Puissance régionale dans l'Indo-Pacifique, alliée des États-Unis et inquiète de la montée en puissance chinoise.
AUS.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Australia]
- Political landscape: PM Malcolm Turnbull (Liberal) took over from Tony Abbott in September 2015 leadership spill; promising innovation-focused agenda.
- Key challenges: End of mining boom, housing affordability crisis in Sydney/Melbourne, asylum seeker policy (offshore detention controversy), climate change impacts on Great Barrier Reef.
- Regional tensions: Balancing economic ties with China against security alliance with US; contributing to anti-ISIS coalition; South China Sea freedom of navigation; Five Eyes member.
- Economic situation: 25th consecutive year without recession, transitioning from mining to services, GDP growth ~2.4%, unemployment around 6%, terms of trade declining.

KOR.name: Republic of Korea
KOR.description: Économie technologique avancée constamment sous la menace nucléaire nord-coréenne.
KOR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — South Korea]
- Political landscape: President Park Geun-hye (Saenuri Party) in office; political landscape stable but growing discontent over economic inequality and chaebol dominance.
- Key challenges: North Korean nuclear threat, aging population, youth unemployment ('Hell Joseon' sentiment), chaebol reform needed, MERS outbreak aftermath from 2015.
- Regional tensions: North Korea's 4th nuclear test on January 6, 2016 heightening security concerns; THAAD missile defense deployment under discussion angering China; comfort women deal with Japan.
- Economic situation: 11th largest economy, ~2.6% growth, export-dependent with Samsung/Hyundai dominant, low inflation, household debt rising.
- Strategic assets: Modern military (625,000 active), US alliance with 28,500 troops stationed, advanced defense industry, top-tier shipbuilding, semiconductor dominance.
- 2016 context: North Korea's January 6 nuclear test triggers emergency response; Kaesong Industrial Complex closure imminent; THAAD negotiations with US accelerating; Park scandal will erupt later in 2016.

MEX.name: Mexico
MEX.description: Économie émergente fortement intégrée aux États-Unis, confrontée aux problèmes des cartels de drogue.
MEX.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Mexico]
- Political landscape: President Enrique Pena Nieto (PRI) facing declining popularity; Ayotzinapa 43 students disappearance (2014) scandal; structural reforms enacted.
- Key challenges: Drug cartel violence (~17,000 homicides in 2015), corruption, impunity, inequality, Chapo Guzman recaptured January 8, 2016; energy reform implementation.
- Economic situation: GDP growth ~2.5%, peso weakening significantly, oil production declining (Pemex struggling), energy reform opening sector to foreign investment; automotive sector booming.
- Regional tensions: US-Mexico relations complicated by Trump candidacy rhetoric; Central American migration through Mexico; cartel violence in border states.

IDN.name: Indonesia
IDN.description: Plus grand archipel du monde et la plus grande économie d'Asie du Sud-Est.
IDN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Indonesia]
- Political landscape: President Joko Widodo ('Jokowi') in second year; reformist agenda facing resistance from old-guard politicians and military; PDI-P coalition governing.
- Key challenges: Deforestation and haze crisis (2015 fires worst in decades), terrorism (ISIS-linked groups), maritime security, Papua separatism, infrastructure deficit, corruption.
- Economic situation: Southeast Asia's largest economy, ~4.9% growth, moderate reforms to attract FDI, infrastructure push, but commodity downturn hurting; rupiah under pressure.
- Regional tensions: South China Sea (Natuna Islands tensions with China), ASEAN's largest member, counter-terrorism cooperation, executing drug traffickers despite international protests.

SAU.name: Saudi Arabia
SAU.description: Premier exportateur mondial de pétrole, en guerre proxy avec l'Iran pour la domination du Moyen-Orient.
SAU.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Saudi Arabia]
- Political landscape: King Salman bin Abdulaziz in power since January 2015; Deputy Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman (MBS) rising rapidly; power consolidation underway.
- Key challenges: Oil price crash (below $30/barrel) devastating revenues; execution of Shia cleric Nimr al-Nimr on January 2, 2016 sparking regional crisis with Iran.
- Regional tensions: Leading military intervention in Yemen (since March 2015); bitter rivalry with Iran intensifying; diplomatic relations severed with Iran January 2016; supporting Syrian rebels.
- Economic situation: Budget deficit of ~15% GDP due to oil crash; beginning austerity measures; subsidy cuts; Vision 2030 economic diversification being planned.
- Strategic assets: World's largest oil exporter, custodian of Mecca and Medina, massive sovereign wealth fund, modern US-supplied military, strategic location.
- 2016 context: MBS preparing Vision 2030 reform plan; mass execution of 47 people on January 2 including Nimr al-Nimr; Saudi embassy in Tehran attacked; OPEC strategy of maintaining production to crush competitors.

TUR.name: Republic of Turkey
TUR.description: Membre de l'OTAN aux ambitions régionales, de plus en plus autoritaire sous Erdoğan, impliquée dans la crise syrienne.
TUR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Turkey]
- Political landscape: President Recep Tayyip Erdogan (AKP) consolidating power after November 2015 election victory; pushing for presidential system; crackdown on media.
- Key challenges: Renewed Kurdish conflict with PKK, ISIS attacks (Ankara bombings October 2015), managing 2.5 million Syrian refugees, press freedom deteriorating.
- Regional tensions: Shot down Russian jet November 2015, severe Russia-Turkey crisis; fighting Kurdish groups in Syria; complex relationship with ISIS on border; EU refugee deal negotiations.
- Economic situation: Growth around 4% but vulnerabilities emerging; lira weakening; tourism sector threatened by terrorism and Russian sanctions; current account deficit.
- Strategic assets: NATO's second-largest army, strategic Bosphorus control, Incirlik Air Base used by US coalition, bridge between Europe and Middle East.
- 2016 context: EU-Turkey refugee deal being negotiated; Erdogan demanding visa liberalization; ISIS suicide bombing in Istanbul Sultanahmet Square January 12, 2016; academics purge beginning.

ITA.name: Italian Republic
ITA.description: Grande puissance européenne fragilisée par une dette massive et une instabilité politique chronique.
ITA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Italy]
- Political landscape: PM Matteo Renzi (PD) pushing constitutional reform referendum; Five Star Movement rising in polls; anti-establishment sentiment growing.
- Key challenges: Economic stagnation, banking crisis (€360B in non-performing loans), migration (170,000 Mediterranean arrivals in 2015), youth unemployment ~37%, mafia influence.
- Economic situation: Third-largest eurozone economy but barely growing (~0.7%); public debt ~132% GDP; banking sector fragility; north-south divide persistent.
- Regional tensions: Frontline of Mediterranean migration crisis (Libya route); NATO ally; Renzi clashing with EU over fiscal flexibility; same-sex civil unions debate.

ESP.name: Kingdom of Spain
ESP.description: Espagne en reprise économique après la crise, confrontée au défi sécessionniste catalan.
ESP.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Spain]
- Political landscape: PM Mariano Rajoy (PP) lost majority in December 2015 elections; Podemos and Ciudadanos disrupted two-party system; government formation crisis; Catalonia independence push.
- Key challenges: Political fragmentation (no government can be formed), Catalonia secession movement, 20.9% unemployment (46% youth), austerity fatigue, corruption scandals.
- Economic situation: Recovery from deep recession with ~3.2% growth, but unemployment still among EU's highest; housing market stabilizing; tourism sector strong.
- Regional tensions: Catalonia declared independence roadmap in November 2015; Basque ETA ceasefire holding; managing migration from Morocco; Gibraltar dispute with UK.

ARG.name: Argentine Republic
ARG.description: Argentine sous le nouveau président Macri tentant de redresser une économie en crise par des réformes libérales.
ARG.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Argentina]
- Political landscape: President Mauricio Macri (Cambiemos) inaugurated December 10, 2015, ending 12 years of Kirchnerism; promising market-friendly reforms and international reengagement.
- Key challenges: Currency controls (cepo cambiario) being dismantled, inflation above 25%, holdout creditor dispute ('vulture funds'), drug trafficking, poverty at ~30%.
- Economic situation: Macri immediately devalued peso by ~40%, lifted capital controls, cut export taxes; seeking to resolve debt holdout crisis and attract investment; GDP stagnant.
- Regional tensions: Falklands/Malvinas sovereignty claim; shifting away from Kirchner-era alliances with Venezuela; rejoining Western-oriented economic policies.

ZAF.name: Republic of South Africa
ZAF.description: Puissance économique de l'Afrique subsaharienne, confrontée à des inégalités massives et une corruption sous Zuma.
ZAF.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — South Africa]
- Political landscape: President Jacob Zuma (ANC) embroiled in scandals; 'Nkandla' homestead corruption ruling pending; #ZumaMustFall movement; opposition DA and EFF gaining ground.
- Key challenges: Extreme inequality (Gini coefficient among world's highest), 25% unemployment, electricity load-shedding crisis (Eskom), violent crime, xenophobic attacks.
- Regional tensions: Diplomatic heavyweight in African Union; mediating in regional conflicts; hosting ICC-wanted Omar al-Bashir controversy in 2015; BRICS membership.
- Economic situation: Near-recession with growth under 1.3%, rand at record lows, ratings downgrade threat, mining sector struggling, structural reform paralysis.
- Strategic assets: Africa's most industrialized economy, advanced financial sector, strategic minerals (platinum, gold, chrome), strong judiciary and constitution, continental diplomatic weight.
- 2016 context: Zuma's finance minister reshuffle in December 2015 caused rand crash; Constitutional Court Nkandla ruling coming (March 2016); student #FeesMustFall protests; drought affecting agriculture.

IRN.name: Islamic Republic of Iran
IRN.description: République islamique sortant des sanctions du JCPOA (accord nucléaire 2015), cherchant à étendre son influence régionale.
IRN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Iran]
- Political landscape: President Hassan Rouhani (moderate); Supreme Leader Ayatollah Khamenei maintaining ultimate authority; reformists vs. hardliners struggle; parliamentary elections upcoming February 2016.
- Key challenges: Implementation of JCPOA nuclear deal, reintegrating into global economy after sanctions, high youth unemployment, human rights concerns, regional overextension.
- Regional tensions: Supporting Assad in Syria, Hezbollah in Lebanon, Houthis in Yemen; rivalry with Saudi Arabia escalating after Nimr execution; influence in Iraq through Shia militias.
- Economic situation: Sanctions relief about to unlock ~$100B in frozen assets; economy contracted under sanctions; inflation declining; oil exports set to increase; population of 80 million.
- Strategic assets: Regional military power, ballistic missile program, Revolutionary Guards (IRGC) and Quds Force, proxy network across Middle East, significant oil/gas reserves.
- 2016 context: JCPOA Implementation Day January 16, 2016 — sanctions lifted, prisoners exchanged; Saudi diplomatic break; positioning to increase oil exports; Rouhani seeking economic dividends from deal.

ISR.name: State of Israel
ISR.description: État très sécuritarisé, puissance régionale et technologique, confronté au conflit israélo-palestinien et à la menace iranienne.
ISR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Israel]
- Political landscape: PM Benjamin Netanyahu leading right-wing coalition; settlement expansion continuing; relations with Obama administration strained over Iran deal.
- Key challenges: Wave of lone-wolf Palestinian stabbing attacks ('Knife Intifada' since October 2015), Gaza blockade tensions, internal divisions over settlements, BDS movement growing.
- Regional tensions: Strongly opposed JCPOA Iran deal; monitoring Hezbollah and Iran in Syria; quiet coordination with Gulf Arab states against Iran; periodic Gaza escalations.
- Economic situation: Strong tech-driven economy (~2.5% growth), low unemployment, but high cost of living and inequality; natural gas discoveries (Leviathan field).
- Strategic assets: Qualitative military edge in region, undeclared nuclear arsenal (~80 warheads), Iron Dome missile defense, elite intelligence services (Mossad, Shin Bet), US $3B annual military aid.
- 2016 context: Knife Intifada continuing; Netanyahu lobbying against Iran deal; Syrian civil war on doorstep with Hezbollah and Iran presence; UN Resolution 2334 on settlements will come later in 2016.

PRK.name: Democratic People's Republic of Korea
PRK.description: Régime totalitaire poursuivant un programme nucléaire en dépit des sanctions. Vient d'effectuer un 4ème essai nucléaire en janvier 2016.
PRK.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — North Korea]
- Political landscape: Supreme Leader Kim Jong-un consolidating power through purges including execution of uncle Jang Song-thaek (2013); cult of personality maintained.
- Key challenges: International isolation, chronic food insecurity, decrepit infrastructure, human rights abuses documented by UN Commission of Inquiry (2014), sanctions pressure.
- Regional tensions: Conducted 4th nuclear test on January 6, 2016 (claimed hydrogen bomb); provocative missile launches; threatening rhetoric toward South Korea and US; Six-Party Talks defunct.
- Economic situation: Command economy heavily dependent on China for trade and aid; black markets growing; sanctions limiting foreign exchange; GDP estimated under $30B.
- Strategic assets: Nuclear weapons program (est. 10-20 warheads), ballistic missile development (Musudan, KN-08), 1.2 million active military, massive artillery aimed at Seoul, cyber warfare capabilities.
- 2016 context: January 6 nuclear test dominates headlines; preparing satellite launch (February 2016); 7th Workers' Party Congress planned for May 2016 (first since 1980); UN Security Council pursuing new sanctions.

UKR.name: Ukraine
UKR.description: Ukraine en guerre depuis l'annexion de la Crimée par la Russie en 2014. Le Donbass est le théâtre de combats continus.
UKR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Ukraine]
- Political landscape: President Petro Poroshenko and PM Arseniy Yatsenyuk governing uneasily; reform fatigue and corruption scandals; Maidan revolution promises partially unfulfilled.
- Key challenges: War in Donbass (over 9,000 dead), Crimea annexed by Russia (2014), massive internal displacement (~1.5M), corruption endemic, economic collapse.
- Regional tensions: Frontline of Russia-West confrontation; Minsk II ceasefire largely holding but no political resolution; energy dependence on Russia; EU Association Agreement being implemented.
- Economic situation: GDP contracted ~9.9% in 2015, currency lost two-thirds of value, inflation ~43%, IMF bailout program with strict conditions, poverty increasing sharply.
- Strategic assets: Strategic location between Russia and EU, significant agricultural potential (breadbasket of Europe), educated workforce, defense industry legacy, gas transit infrastructure.
- 2016 context: EU visa liberalization process advancing; trade provisions of EU Association Agreement entering force January 1, 2016; Russia retaliating with trade embargo; Donbass conflict at stalemate.

POL.name: Republic of Poland
POL.description: Pologne sous le gouvernement nationaliste PiS, fer de lance de l'opposition à la Russie au sein de l'UE.
POL.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Poland]
- Political landscape: President Andrzej Duda and PM Beata Szydlo (PiS, Law and Justice) newly in power since late 2015; pushing conservative nationalist agenda; Constitutional Tribunal crisis.
- Key challenges: Rule of law concerns (packing Constitutional Tribunal), EU confrontation over democratic backsliding, refusing refugee quotas, media law changes, brain drain.
- Economic situation: Largest Central European economy, ~3.6% growth, unemployment declining, EU structural funds driving investment; not in eurozone; manufacturing and services strong.
- Regional tensions: Pushing for stronger NATO eastern presence against Russia; requesting permanent US bases; close Visegrad Group (V4) cooperation; Lithuania and Ukraine solidarity.

NLD.name: Netherlands
NLD.description: Petite puissance économique avec un poids diplomatique considérable grâce à Rotterdam et ses institutions financières.
NLD.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Netherlands]
- Political landscape: PM Mark Rutte (VVD) in coalition with PvdA; EU referendum on Ukraine Association Agreement coming April 2016; PVV (Geert Wilders) rising in polls.
- Key challenges: Integration of migrants, housing crisis, MH17 investigation (shot down over Ukraine July 2014), Geert Wilders on trial for incitement, climate vulnerability (below sea level).
- Economic situation: Strong recovery with ~2% growth, low unemployment, major trading nation (Rotterdam port), gas revenues declining (Groningen earthquakes limiting production).
- Regional tensions: Leading MH17 investigation implicating Russia; hosting International Criminal Court and tribunals; EU founding member; Curacao/Aruba/Sint Maarten kingdom relations.

CHE.name: Swiss Confederation
CHE.description: La Suisse, bastion de la neutralité, abrite le CICR, l'ONU-Genève et les grandes banques mondiales.
CHE.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Switzerland]
- Political landscape: Federal Council consensus government; direct democracy system; 2014 immigration initiative complicating EU relations; SVP (right-wing populist) largest party.
- Key challenges: Managing immigration quotas vs. EU bilateral agreements, strong franc hurting exporters, banking secrecy under international pressure, aging population.
- Economic situation: Wealthy nation (~$80K GDP per capita), SNB maintaining negative interest rates after January 2015 franc shock; pharmaceuticals, banking, and precision manufacturing dominant.
- Regional tensions: Neutral status; hosting international organizations (UN Geneva, ICRC); negotiating with EU on immigration framework agreement.

SWE.name: Kingdom of Sweden
SWE.description: Suède à la politique étrangère humaniste, accueillant le plus grand nombre de réfugiés syriens en 2016.
SWE.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Sweden]
- Political landscape: PM Stefan Lofven (Social Democrats) leading minority government; record 163,000 asylum seekers in 2015 overwhelming capacity; border controls reimposed.
- Key challenges: Refugee integration crisis, housing shortage, gang violence in suburbs, maintaining welfare state with migration costs, defense spending debate (Russian submarine incidents).
- Economic situation: Strong growth (~4.1%), tech and innovation hub (Spotify, Ericsson), but welfare model strained; not in eurozone; low public debt.

NOR.name: Kingdom of Norway
NOR.description: Norvège riche en pétrole, très active en diplomatie et humanitaire, membre fondateur de l'OTAN.
NOR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Norway]
- Political landscape: PM Erna Solberg (Conservative) in minority coalition; oil price drop testing Norwegian model; refugee arrivals surged in 2015.
- Key challenges: Oil-dependent economy adjusting to lower prices, Arctic strategy, refugee integration, maintaining welfare state, climate change commitments.
- Economic situation: Wealthy ($74K GDP per capita) but mainland GDP growth slowing to ~1%; Government Pension Fund Global (world's largest sovereign wealth fund, ~$830B) providing buffer.

PAK.name: Islamic Republic of Pakistan
PAK.description: Puissance nucléaire en Asie du Sud, dans une relation complexe avec l'Inde et les États-Unis, et la menace talibane.
PAK.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Pakistan]
- Political landscape: PM Nawaz Sharif (PML-N) governing; military under Gen. Raheel Sharif maintaining significant influence; civil-military tensions over foreign policy and counterterrorism.
- Key challenges: Terrorism (TTP, sectarian groups), endemic corruption, energy crisis with chronic power shortages, poverty, education deficit, Balochistan insurgency.
- Regional tensions: Complex relationship with Afghanistan and Taliban; India tensions spiking after Pathankot attack (January 2016); China-Pakistan Economic Corridor (CPEC) developing; nuclear arsenal growing.
- Economic situation: GDP growth around 4.2%, IMF program improving macroeconomic stability, but structural issues persist; CPEC investment promising $46B in infrastructure.
- Strategic assets: Nuclear arsenal (~130 warheads, fastest-growing), 650,000 active military, strategic location between South and Central Asia, intelligence services (ISI).
- 2016 context: National Action Plan against terrorism after 2014 Peshawar school massacre; Zarb-e-Azb military operation in tribal areas; Pathankot attack January 2 threatens India-Pakistan dialogue.

NGA.name: Federal Republic of Nigeria
NGA.description: Plus grande économie d'Afrique en récession à cause de la chute des prix du pétrole, combattant Boko Haram.
NGA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Nigeria]
- Political landscape: President Muhammadu Buhari (APC) in first year after historic democratic transition from Goodluck Jonathan; anti-corruption drive launched.
- Key challenges: Boko Haram insurgency in northeast (Chibok girls still missing), Niger Delta militancy threatening oil production, electricity crisis, endemic corruption.
- Regional tensions: Leading multinational force against Boko Haram; tensions in oil-rich Niger Delta; competition with South Africa for continental leadership; Lake Chad humanitarian crisis.
- Economic situation: Africa's largest economy but oil price crash devastating revenues (oil = 70% government income); naira under pressure; inflation rising; diversification urgently needed.
- Strategic assets: Largest population in Africa (~180M), largest African economy, significant oil reserves, regional military power (ECOWAS leadership), large diaspora.
- 2016 context: Buhari's anti-corruption campaign targeting predecessor's allies; Boko Haram weakened but still deadly; fuel shortages; budget delayed by oil revenue shortfall; Niger Delta Avengers emerging.

EGY.name: Arab Republic of Egypt
EGY.description: Égypte sous al-Sissi après le coup d'état de 2013, cherchant la stabilité dans une région en feu.
EGY.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Egypt]
- Political landscape: President Abdel Fattah el-Sisi consolidating authoritarian rule after 2013 military coup; Muslim Brotherhood crushed; mass political imprisonments.
- Key challenges: Sinai insurgency (ISIS affiliate Wilayat Sinai), human rights crackdown, youth unemployment, water security (Ethiopian GERD dam dispute), press freedom eliminated.
- Regional tensions: Sinai insurgency including downing of Russian Metrojet Flight 9268 (October 2015); Libya instability on western border; mediating in Palestinian affairs; supporting Haftar in Libya.
- Economic situation: Growth ~4.2% but unsustainable subsidy regime, currency overvaluation, foreign reserves declining, tourism devastated by terrorism and Russian flight ban.
- Strategic assets: Largest Arab military, Suez Canal (expanded August 2015), US military aid (~$1.3B/year), strategic location, diplomatic weight as most populous Arab state.
- 2016 context: Italian researcher Giulio Regeni disappeared January 25, 2016 (found murdered); new Suez Canal channel operating; currency devaluation looming; mega-projects to boost economy.

VNM.name: Socialist Republic of Vietnam
VNM.description: Vietnam se rapprochant des États-Unis tout en gérant des tensions persistantes avec la Chine en mer de Chine.
VNM.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Vietnam]
- Political landscape: Communist Party Congress upcoming January 2016 to select new leadership; General Secretary Nguyen Phu Trong consolidating; one-party state with factional politics.
- Key challenges: South China Sea confrontation with China (oil rig crisis 2014), corruption, environmental degradation, SOE reform, press freedom, rapid urbanization.
- Economic situation: Growth ~6.7%, manufacturing hub (Samsung largest exporter), FDI flowing in (TPP signatory), young workforce, poverty reduction success story.
- Regional tensions: Major South China Sea claimant; US rapprochement accelerating (arms embargo lifted partially); ASEAN member; balancing China and US relations.

PHL.name: Republic of the Philippines
PHL.description: Philippines contestant la Chine en mer de Chine méridionale devant le Tribunal international de La Haye.
PHL.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Philippines]
- Political landscape: President Benigno Aquino III in final year; May 2016 elections upcoming (Duterte, Poe, Roxas, Binay competing); good governance reform agenda.
- Key challenges: South China Sea dispute (arbitration case pending at PCA), Mindanao insurgencies (MILF peace process, Abu Sayyaf), typhoon vulnerability, poverty, corruption.
- Economic situation: Strong growth ~5.9%, BPO sector booming, remittances from ~10M overseas workers vital, infrastructure deficit, rising middle class.
- Regional tensions: South China Sea arbitration case against China expected ruling July 2016; US alliance strengthening; Enhanced Defense Cooperation Agreement with US.

COL.name: Republic of Colombia
COL.description: Colombie en cours de négociations historiques de paix avec les FARC, soutenu par les États-Unis.
COL.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Colombia]
- Political landscape: President Juan Manuel Santos pursuing historic peace negotiations with FARC guerrillas in Havana; ELN talks also being explored; public opinion divided.
- Key challenges: FARC peace process at critical stage, coca cultivation rising, displaced population (~6.9M, world's largest), paramilitary successor groups (BACRIM).
- Economic situation: Growth slowing to ~3.1% from oil price decline (oil = major export); peso depreciating sharply; inflation rising; Pacific Alliance member.
- Regional tensions: Venezuelan border tensions (border closed August-December 2015); US partner in counter-narcotics (Plan Colombia legacy); Ecuador border management.

VEN.name: Bolivarian Republic of Venezuela
VEN.description: Venezuela en grave crise économique et politique sous Maduro, avec une inflation hyperbolique et des pénuries alimentaires.
VEN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Venezuela]
- Political landscape: President Nicolas Maduro (PSUV) facing hostile National Assembly after opposition MUD won supermajority in December 2015 elections; constitutional crisis brewing.
- Key challenges: Economic collapse (hyperinflation emerging, severe shortages of food and medicine), crime (world's highest murder rate), political polarization, oil dependence.
- Economic situation: GDP contracting ~6%, inflation above 180%, currency in freefall, oil revenues collapsed, foreign reserves critical; humanitarian crisis emerging.
- Regional tensions: OAS tensions over democratic governance; Cuba ally under pressure; Colombian border disputes; losing Petrocaribe leverage as oil revenues collapse.

IRQ.name: Republic of Iraq
IRQ.description: Irak en guerre contre l'État islamique (Daesh) qui contrôle Mossoul et de larges territoires au nord et à l'ouest.
IRQ.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Iraq]
- Political landscape: PM Haider al-Abadi leading fragile government; ISIS controlling Mosul and western Anbar; Shia-Sunni-Kurdish power-sharing under extreme strain.
- Key challenges: ISIS insurgency (second-largest city Mosul under ISIS since June 2014), sectarian divisions, Kurdish autonomy push, Shia militia power, massive displacement (~3.3M IDPs).
- Economic situation: Oil-dependent economy devastated by war and low oil prices; budget crisis; reconstruction needs enormous; southern oil fields still producing.
- Regional tensions: US-led coalition conducting airstrikes; Iran-backed militias fighting ISIS alongside army; Kurdish Peshmerga holding frontlines; Turkey's unauthorized military presence in Bashiqa.

SYR.name: Syrian Arab Republic
SYR.description: Syrie déchirée par 5 ans de guerre civile entre Assad, les rebelles, l'État islamique et les Kurdes.
SYR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Syria]
- Political landscape: President Bashar al-Assad controlling ~20% of territory with Russian and Iranian support; opposition fragmented; ISIS holding large eastern territories.
- Key challenges: Catastrophic civil war (250,000+ dead, half population displaced), ISIS territorial control, humanitarian crisis, chemical weapons use, refugee exodus (4.5M abroad).
- Economic situation: Economy destroyed (GDP contracted ~60% since 2011); infrastructure devastated; besieged populations facing starvation; war economy and sanctions.
- Regional tensions: Russia intervening militarily since September 2015; Iran and Hezbollah supporting Assad; Turkey, Gulf states backing rebels; US-led coalition targeting ISIS; Vienna peace talks ongoing.

ARE.name: United Arab Emirates
ARE.description: Émirats Arabes Unis, hub financier et diplomatique du Golfe, engagé militairement au Yémen.
ARE.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — United Arab Emirates]
- Political landscape: Federation of seven emirates led by Abu Dhabi's Sheikh Khalifa bin Zayed (incapacitated; de facto rule by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Zayed); Dubai as commercial hub.
- Key challenges: Oil price decline affecting Abu Dhabi revenues, diversification through Dubai model, labor rights concerns for migrant workers, regional instability.
- Regional tensions: Participating in Saudi-led Yemen coalition, strong anti-Iran stance, supporting Haftar in Libya, part of anti-ISIS coalition.
- Economic situation: Diversified by Gulf standards; Dubai's tourism, finance, and logistics sectors growing; Abu Dhabi oil wealth providing fiscal buffer; Expo 2020 preparations.

QAT.name: State of Qatar
QAT.description: Qatar, plus riche pays du monde per capita, mène une diplomatie active via Al Jazeera et son soutien aux Frères Musulmans.
QAT.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Qatar]
- Political landscape: Emir Tamim bin Hamad Al Thani ruling since 2013; small but outsized diplomatic influence; Al Jazeera media network as soft power tool.
- Key challenges: 2022 FIFA World Cup construction (migrant worker deaths and exploitation), kafala labor system, oil/gas price decline, diplomatic balancing act.
- Economic situation: World's highest GDP per capita; LNG exports cushioning oil price decline better than neighbors; massive infrastructure spending for World Cup; QIA sovereign wealth fund.

CUB.name: Republic of Cuba
CUB.description: Cuba en cours de normalisation avec les États-Unis depuis 2014, mais l'embargo est maintenu. Raúl Castro dirige.
CUB.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Cuba]
- Political landscape: President Raul Castro leading gradual reforms; historic US-Cuba normalization underway since December 2014; Obama planning visit (March 2016).
- Key challenges: Economic reform pace slow, dual currency system, crumbling infrastructure, internet access limited, political freedoms restricted, emigration pressure.
- Economic situation: GDP growth ~4.3% but from low base; tourism booming due to US normalization; remittances important; state still controls most economy; Venezuela oil subsidies declining.
- Regional tensions: Hosting FARC peace talks; improving US relations but embargo largely intact pending Congress; declining Venezuelan support as Maduro regime struggles.

KAZ.name: Republic of Kazakhstan
KAZ.description: Kazakhstan équilibrant ses relations entre Russie, Chine et Occident, riche en ressources naturelles.
KAZ.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Kazakhstan]
- Political landscape: President Nursultan Nazarbayev in power since 1989 (Soviet era); authoritarian stability; Astana as showcase capital; succession question unaddressed.
- Key challenges: Oil price collapse hitting economy, tenge dramatically devalued August 2015, diversification from oil, corruption, political freedom absent, labor unrest.
- Economic situation: Central Asia's largest economy; growth slowed to ~1.2% from oil crash; tenge lost 45% in 2015; major oil producer (Kashagan field finally starting); Chinese investment growing.
- Regional tensions: Balancing between Russia and China; member of Eurasian Economic Union; hosting Syria peace talks in Astana; Baikonur Cosmodrome leased to Russia.

THA.name: Kingdom of Thailand
THA.description: Thaïlande sous junte militaire depuis 2014, se rapprochant de la Chine après la critique occidentale du coup d'état.
THA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Thailand]
- Political landscape: Military junta (NCPO) under PM Prayut Chan-o-cha since May 2014 coup; martial law replaced by Article 44; constitution drafting; King Bhumibol Adulyadej ailing.
- Key challenges: Stalled democracy, lese-majeste law enforcement, southern Malay-Muslim insurgency, aging king creating succession anxiety, political polarization (Red vs Yellow).
- Economic situation: Growth ~2.9% recovering slowly from coup disruption; tourism resilient; automotive and electronics manufacturing; rice exports; baht relatively stable.
- Regional tensions: Mekong River dam disputes with Laos and China; South China Sea not direct claimant but ASEAN implications; military ties with both US and China.

MYS.name: Malaysia
MYS.description: Malaisie affectée par le scandale de corruption 1MDB impliquant le PM Najib Razak.
MYS.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Malaysia]
- Political landscape: PM Najib Razak (UMNO/BN) embroiled in 1MDB scandal (billions allegedly siphoned from state fund); opposition weakened after Anwar Ibrahim imprisonment.
- Key challenges: 1MDB corruption scandal ($681M found in Najib's personal accounts), ethnic tensions (Malay-Chinese-Indian), GST implementation backlash, press freedom declining.
- Economic situation: Growth ~5% but ringgit fell sharply; oil and palm oil exports declining; manufacturing and electronics strong; middle-income trap concerns.
- Regional tensions: South China Sea claimant; MH370 mystery unresolved; hosting Rohingya refugees; ASEAN member; IS-linked extremism concerns.

ETH.name: Federal Democratic Republic of Ethiopia
ETH.description: Éthiopie avec l'une des économies à plus forte croissance d'Afrique, mais réprimant les protestations internes.
ETH.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Ethiopia]
- Political landscape: PM Hailemariam Desalegn (EPRDF) governing; ruling coalition won 100% of parliamentary seats in 2015; authoritarian developmental state model.
- Key challenges: Oromo protests erupting since November 2015 over Addis Ababa master plan (land grabs), ethnic tensions, press freedom crackdown, drought emergency.
- Economic situation: One of world's fastest-growing economies (~10% growth), but from low base; Grand Ethiopian Renaissance Dam (GERD) under construction; Chinese-funded infrastructure boom.
- Regional tensions: Troops in Somalia (AMISOM peacekeeping), GERD dispute with Egypt and Sudan over Nile waters, hosting ~700,000 refugees (South Sudan, Eritrea, Somalia).

GRC.name: Hellenic Republic
GRC.description: Grèce sous austérité sévère après la crise de la dette, avec un chômage record et une crise humanitaire migratoire.
GRC.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Greece]
- Political landscape: PM Alexis Tsipras (Syriza) won September 2015 snap election; implementing third bailout despite anti-austerity mandate; opposition weakened.
- Key challenges: Debt crisis (debt ~175% GDP), third bailout implementation, refugee crisis (over 850,000 arrivals in 2015 via islands), 25% unemployment, brain drain.
- Economic situation: GDP contracted ~0.2%, six years of depression wiped out 25% of GDP; capital controls since June 2015; banking sector fragile; tourism providing lifeline.
- Regional tensions: Primary entry point for refugees into EU; tensions with Turkey over Aegean; Cyprus dispute; Macedonia naming dispute; NATO member despite economic crisis.

CZE.name: Czech Republic
CZE.description: République Tchèque, économie performante et opposition marquée aux quotas de réfugiés de l'UE.
CZE.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Czech Republic]
- Political landscape: PM Bohuslav Sobotka (CSSD) leading coalition; President Milos Zeman making controversial pro-Russian and anti-refugee statements; strong anti-immigration sentiment.
- Key challenges: Refusing EU refugee quotas, Zeman's divisive presidency, brain drain to Western Europe, housing shortage in Prague, cybersecurity threats.
- Economic situation: Strong performer with ~4.5% GDP growth, low unemployment (~4.5%), automotive industry (Skoda) driving exports, resisting euro adoption.

HUN.name: Hungary
HUN.description: Hongrie d'Orbán, pionnier de la démocratie illibérale en Europe, construisant des clôtures contre les réfugiés.
HUN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Hungary]
- Political landscape: PM Viktor Orban (Fidesz) pursuing 'illiberal democracy'; built border fence against refugees in 2015; clashing with EU over rule of law and refugee quotas.
- Key challenges: Rule of law concerns (media control, judicial independence), refugee policy confrontation with EU, Roma marginalization, brain drain, demographic decline.
- Economic situation: Growth ~3.1%, manufacturing (automotive) strong, unemployment low but wages stagnant; Orban's economic nationalism including bank levies and utility price cuts.
- Regional tensions: Blocking EU refugee quotas; close ties with Putin's Russia (Paks nuclear deal, South Stream); advocating for ethnic Hungarian minorities in neighbors.

ROU.name: Romania
ROU.description: Roumanie très pro-OTAN et pro-UE, inquiète des ambitions russes après l'Ukraine.
ROU.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Romania]
- Political landscape: PM Dacian Ciolos leading technocratic government after PM Ponta resigned November 2015 following Colectiv nightclub fire protests; anti-corruption momentum.
- Key challenges: Corruption (anti-corruption agency DNA active), emigration (~3M abroad), infrastructure deficit, Roma marginalization, judicial reform, poverty in rural areas.
- Economic situation: Growth ~3.7%, EU's fastest-growing large economy; IT sector booming (Bucharest, Cluj); EU structural funds underutilized; fiscal space from low debt.

AUT.name: Republic of Austria
AUT.description: Petite démocratie alpine au cœur de l'Europe, traditionnellement neutre mais profondément intégrée dans l'Union Européenne.
AUT.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Austria]
- Political landscape: Coalition government (SPO-OVP) under Chancellor Werner Faymann; far-right FPO surging in polls over refugee crisis; presidential election upcoming.
- Key challenges: Refugee influx (90,000 asylum applications in 2015 for population of 8.7M), border controls reimposed, rising anti-immigration sentiment.
- Economic situation: Stable economy with ~1% growth, well-developed welfare state, banking sector exposed to Eastern Europe, tourism important sector.
- Regional tensions: Key transit country on Balkan refugee route; tensions with Hungary over border management; Brenner Pass border controls with Italy being discussed.

BEL.name: Kingdom of Belgium
BEL.description: Siège de l'OTAN et des institutions européennes, la Belgique est au cœur de la gouvernance occidentale malgré ses tensions communautaires internes.
BEL.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Belgium]
- Political landscape: PM Charles Michel (MR) leading center-right coalition; heightened security after November 2015 Brussels lockdown linked to Paris attacks; Molenbeek under scrutiny.
- Key challenges: Terrorism threat (Paris attackers had Belgian connections), linguistic community divisions (Flemish-Walloon), radicalization in Brussels neighborhoods.
- Economic situation: Moderate growth (~1.4%), high public debt (~106% GDP), complex federal structure raising governance costs; NATO and EU headquarters host.
- Regional tensions: Heart of EU institutions, Belgium increasingly focused on domestic security; Salah Abdeslam (Paris attacks suspect) being hunted in Brussels as of January 2016.

DNK.name: Kingdom of Denmark
DNK.description: Monarchie nordique prospère, membre actif de l'OTAN et de l'UE, le Danemark jouit d'une des meilleures qualités de vie mondiales.
DNK.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Denmark]
- Political landscape: PM Lars Lokke Rasmussen (Venstre) leading minority government with Danish People's Party support; anti-immigration policies tightening.
- Key challenges: Refugee crisis response, integration of immigrants, welfare state sustainability, Greenland self-governance aspirations, Arctic strategy.
- Economic situation: Wealthy economy (~$53K GDP per capita), ~1.2% growth, strong welfare state, low unemployment; passed controversial law allowing seizure of asylum seekers' valuables.

FIN.name: Republic of Finland
FIN.description: Démocratie nordique partageant une longue frontière avec la Russie, la Finlande maintient une politique de non-alignement militaire tout en s'intégrant profondément à l'UE.
FIN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Finland]
- Political landscape: PM Juha Sipila (Centre Party) leading center-right government; implementing austerity and competitiveness reforms; social compact under strain.
- Key challenges: Economic stagnation (Nokia decline, Russia sanctions impact, paper industry decline), asylum seeker influx (32,000 in 2015), aging population, competitiveness gap.
- Economic situation: GDP growth near zero; recession in 2012-2014 lingering; tech sector rebuilding post-Nokia; forestry and engineering exports still important; eurozone member.

IRL.name: Republic of Ireland
IRL.description: Petit État insulaire à l'économie dynamique, carrefour entre l'Europe et le monde anglophone, l'Irlande attire massivement les multinationales technologiques.
IRL.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Ireland]
- Political landscape: Taoiseach Enda Kenny (Fine Gael) in coalition with Labour; general election imminent (February 2016); Celtic Tiger recovery boosting government popularity.
- Key challenges: Housing crisis and homelessness in Dublin, healthcare system strain, rural-urban divide, water charges controversy, Northern Ireland border post-Brexit concerns.
- Economic situation: EU's fastest-growing economy (~26% GDP growth in 2015, partly statistical artifact from corporate relocations); tech multinationals' European hub; exited bailout in 2013.

PRT.name: Portuguese Republic
PRT.description: Ancienne puissance coloniale reconvertie en démocratie atlantiste, le Portugal maintient des liens culturels forts avec ses ex-colonies lusophones.
PRT.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Portugal]
- Political landscape: PM Antonio Costa (PS) newly in government since November 2015 with Left Bloc and Communist support; reversing austerity measures; President Cavaco Silva skeptical.
- Key challenges: High public debt (~129% GDP), unemployment ~12.4%, emigration (especially youth), banking sector fragility, aging population, forest fire vulnerability.
- Economic situation: Post-bailout recovery with ~1.5% growth; tourism booming (Lisbon, Porto); exited EU/IMF bailout 2014 but debt sustainability concerns persist.

BGR.name: Republic of Bulgaria
BGR.description: Membre de l'OTAN et de l'UE, la Bulgarie reste économiquement liée à la Russie par son énergie et maintient une position ambiguë entre Orient et Occident.
BGR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Bulgaria]
- Political landscape: PM Boyko Borisov (GERB) leading coalition; EU's poorest member state struggling with corruption and judicial reform; refugee pressure on Turkish border.
- Key challenges: Population decline (emigration and low birth rate), corruption, organized crime, Roma minority marginalization, energy dependence on Russia.
- Economic situation: Modest growth (~3%), low wages (EU's lowest), currency board pegged to euro, fiscal discipline maintained, EU funds important for development.

HRV.name: Republic of Croatia
HRV.description: Jeune démocratie balkanique intégrée à l'OTAN et à l'UE, la Croatie capitalise sur son littoral adriatique pour développer un tourisme de premier plan.
HRV.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Croatia]
- Political landscape: PM Tihomir Oreskovic newly appointed January 2016 as technocratic PM in HDZ-MOST coalition; political instability from fragmented parliament.
- Key challenges: High unemployment (~16%), brain drain to Western EU, war legacy issues with Serbia, refugee transit route management, Adriatic tourism dependence.
- Economic situation: EU's newest member (2013), emerging from six-year recession with ~1.6% growth; tourism driving recovery; public debt above 85% GDP; not yet in eurozone.

SRB.name: Republic of Serbia
SRB.description: Carrefour des Balkans cherchant à équilibrer son rapprochement européen avec ses liens traditionnels avec Moscou, la Serbie refuse de rejoindre l'OTAN.
SRB.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Serbia]
- Political landscape: PM Aleksandar Vucic (SNS) dominating politics; EU accession negotiations opened; balancing Western integration with Russian and Chinese ties.
- Key challenges: Kosovo status unresolved, EU accession conditions (rule of law, Kosovo normalization), media freedom concerns, unemployment, brain drain.
- Economic situation: Growth ~0.7%, IMF program supporting reforms, FDI increasing, automotive sector growing; public debt ~76% GDP; gradual recovery from years of stagnation.
- Regional tensions: Kosovo normalization dialogue (Brussels process); not joining EU sanctions on Russia; refugee transit route; Vucic consolidating media control.

BLR.name: Republic of Belarus
BLR.description: Dernière dictature d'Europe selon l'Occident, la Biélorussie est l'alliée la plus fidèle de Moscou et dépend économiquement de la Russie.
BLR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Belarus]
- Political landscape: President Alexander Lukashenko in power since 1994 ('Europe's last dictator'); released political prisoners in August 2015 leading to partial EU sanctions relief.
- Key challenges: Authoritarian governance, economic dependence on Russia, limited diversification, brain drain of educated youth, human rights concerns.
- Economic situation: GDP contracting (~-3.8%), heavily subsidized by Russian energy discounts, state-dominated economy, inflation concerns, currency instability.
- Regional tensions: Mediating role in Ukraine conflict (Minsk agreements hosted in Belarus); balancing between Russia and cautious EU reengagement; member of Eurasian Economic Union.

GEO.name: Georgia
GEO.description: Petit État du Caucase aspirant à rejoindre l'OTAN et l'UE, la Géorgie a subi une guerre éclair avec la Russie en 2008 et tente de résister à l'influence moscovite.
GEO.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Georgia]
- Political landscape: PM Irakli Garibashvili (Georgian Dream); EU Association Agreement signed, pro-Western orientation; Saakashvili-era UNM in opposition.
- Key challenges: Russian occupation of Abkhazia and South Ossetia (20% of territory), NATO membership aspiration blocked by Russia, judicial reform, poverty.
- Economic situation: Growth ~2.8%, hit by Russian recession reducing remittances and trade; tourism growing; lari depreciation; EU DCFTA being implemented.
- Regional tensions: 'Borderization' by Russia in occupied territories; NATO aspirations without Membership Action Plan; transit role for Caucasus energy corridor (BTC, BTE pipelines).

AZE.name: Republic of Azerbaijan
AZE.description: République pétrolière du Caucase gouvernée autoritairement, l'Azerbaïdjan joue un rôle énergétique clé pour l'Europe et est en conflit gelé avec l'Arménie au Haut-Karabakh.
AZE.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Azerbaijan]
- Political landscape: President Ilham Aliyev in authoritarian rule since 2003; crackdown on civil society, journalists, and opposition activists intensified.
- Key challenges: Oil price collapse devastating petro-state economy, manat devaluation, Nagorno-Karabakh conflict with Armenia, human rights deterioration.
- Economic situation: Manat lost half its value in 2015 devaluations; oil revenues (90% of exports) collapsing; sovereign wealth fund (SOFAZ) being drawn down; banking crisis emerging.
- Regional tensions: Nagorno-Karabakh ceasefire line increasingly volatile; energy corridor to Europe (BTC pipeline, TANAP); balancing Russia, Turkey, and Iran relations.

ARM.name: Republic of Armenia
ARM.description: Petit État enclavé du Caucase, allié militaire de la Russie tout en maintenant des liens diasporiques avec la France et les États-Unis, engagé dans un conflit avec l'Azerbaïdjan.
ARM.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Armenia]
- Political landscape: President Serzh Sargsyan (Republican Party); constitutional referendum in December 2015 approved shift to parliamentary system; opposition alleging power consolidation.
- Key challenges: Nagorno-Karabakh frozen conflict with Azerbaijan, economic stagnation, emigration (population declining), Russian dependence, blockaded borders with Turkey and Azerbaijan.
- Regional tensions: Nagorno-Karabakh Line of Contact incidents increasing; member of Russian-led CSTO and Eurasian Economic Union; Turkish border closed since 1993.
- Economic situation: Small economy (~$10.5B GDP), remittance-dependent, hit by Russian recession reducing remittances; mining sector important; poverty around 30%.

SVK.name: Slovak Republic
SVK.description: Économie d'Europe centrale dynamique grâce à son industrie automobile, la Slovaquie adopte parfois des positions eurosceptiques sur les questions migratoires.
SVK.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Slovakia]
- Political landscape: PM Robert Fico (Smer-SD) heading into March 2016 elections; strongly opposing EU refugee quotas; anti-immigration rhetoric intensifying.
- Key challenges: Anti-Muslim/refugee sentiment despite minimal refugee presence, Roma marginalization, corruption, brain drain, automotive industry over-dependence.
- Economic situation: Growth ~3.6%, automotive industry driving exports (VW, Kia, Peugeot), eurozone member, low unemployment; Visegrad Group coordination on EU policy.

SVN.name: Republic of Slovenia
SVN.description: La plus prospère des républiques issues de l'ex-Yougoslavie, la Slovénie est pleinement intégrée à l'UE et à l'OTAN et sert de pont entre l'Europe centrale et les Balkans.
SVN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Slovenia]
- Political landscape: PM Miro Cerar (SMC) leading coalition; small but stable EU member; refugee transit route causing tensions in 2015.
- Key challenges: Banking sector restructured after 2013 bailout, refugee transit management (fence built on Croatian border), aging population, fiscal consolidation.
- Economic situation: Recovery with ~2.3% growth after deep banking crisis; export-oriented (pharmaceuticals, automotive); eurozone member; tourism (Ljubljana, Lake Bled) growing.

LTU.name: Republic of Lithuania
LTU.description: La plus grande des républiques baltes, la Lituanie est une farouche partisane de l'OTAN et de l'UE face à ce qu'elle perçoit comme une menace existentielle russe.
LTU.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Lithuania]
- Political landscape: President Dalia Grybauskaite; PM Algirdas Butkevicius (Social Democrats); strong Euro-Atlantic orientation; adopted euro January 2015.
- Key challenges: Russian security threat (Kaliningrad proximity), emigration (population dropped from 3.5M to 2.9M since independence), demographic decline, energy independence.
- Economic situation: Growth ~1.8%, eurozone's newest member, building Klaipeda LNG terminal to reduce Russian gas dependence; IT sector growing.

LVA.name: Republic of Latvia
LVA.description: République baltique stratégique avec une importante minorité russophone, la Lettonie est profondément atlantiste et cherche à renforcer la présence de l'OTAN sur son territoire.
LVA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Latvia]
- Political landscape: PM Laimdota Straujuma (Unity) leading coalition; large Russian-speaking minority (~25%); strong NATO and EU orientation.
- Key challenges: Russian-speaking minority integration, Russian hybrid warfare concerns, emigration and population decline, income inequality, banking sector (non-resident deposits).
- Economic situation: Post-crisis recovery with ~2.7% growth; eurozone member since 2014; IT and logistics sectors; Riga as regional hub; EU structural funds important.

EST.name: Republic of Estonia
EST.description: Pionnière du gouvernement numérique, l'Estonie consacre 2% de son PIB à la défense et est l'un des membres les plus actifs de l'OTAN face à la Russie.
EST.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Estonia]
- Political landscape: PM Taavi Roivas (Reform Party) leading coalition; digital governance pioneer (e-Residency program); strong Atlanticist and EU orientation.
- Key challenges: Russian-speaking minority integration (25% of population), Russian hybrid warfare concerns, emigration, defense spending pressure from NATO commitment.
- Economic situation: Small open economy, ~1.4% growth, eurozone member, strong IT sector, low public debt; transitioning from low-cost to innovation economy.

MKD.name: Republic of Macedonia
MKD.description: Petit État balkanique enclavé dont l'adhésion à l'OTAN est bloquée par la Grèce en raison d'un différend sur le nom du pays, la Macédoine cherche à se rapprocher de l'Occident.
MKD.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Macedonia (FYROM)]
- Political landscape: PM Nikola Gruevski (VMRO-DPMNE) clinging to power amid political crisis; wiretapping scandal revealed massive surveillance; EU-brokered Przino Agreement.
- Key challenges: Political crisis from 2015 wiretapping scandal, name dispute with Greece blocking EU/NATO accession, ethnic Albanian minority tensions, corruption.
- Economic situation: Small economy with ~3.7% growth, high unemployment (~26%), EU candidate since 2005 but no start of accession talks; foreign investment limited by political instability.

DZA.name: People's Democratic Republic of Algeria
DZA.description: Plus grand pays d'Afrique par superficie, l'Algérie est une puissance régionale pétrolière qui maintient une politique étrangère de non-alignement et des relations complexes avec la France.
DZA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Algeria]
- Political landscape: President Abdelaziz Bouteflika in poor health (rarely seen publicly since 2013 stroke); power exercised by military/security establishment ('le pouvoir').
- Key challenges: Youth unemployment, housing crisis, governance opacity, Bouteflika succession question, terrorism risk from Sahel instability, south Libya spillover.
- Economic situation: Oil and gas revenues (95% exports) collapsing; foreign reserves declining rapidly; budget deficit; resistance to IMF-style reforms; large hydrocarbon reserves.
- Regional tensions: Rivalry with Morocco over Western Sahara; border security concerns from Libya and Mali; In Amenas gas plant attack (2013) legacy; not joining regional military operations.

MAR.name: Kingdom of Morocco
MAR.description: Monarchie stable et modérée du Maghreb, le Maroc est un partenaire privilégié de l'Occident en Afrique du Nord et revendique la souveraineté sur le Sahara Occidental.
MAR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Morocco]
- Political landscape: King Mohammed VI as executive monarch; PM Abdelilah Benkirane (PJD, moderate Islamist); relatively stable after limited Arab Spring reforms.
- Key challenges: Youth unemployment, rural poverty, education quality, Western Sahara dispute, terrorism risk, irregular migration to Europe.
- Economic situation: Growth ~4.5%, phosphate exports, agriculture, automotive and aerospace industry growing (Renault, Bombardier), tourism; Tangier Med port expanding.
- Regional tensions: Western Sahara sovereignty dispute with Polisario Front (backed by Algeria); UN peacekeeping mission MINURSO; Morocco left African Union in 1984 (rejoined 2017).

TUN.name: Tunisian Republic
TUN.description: Seul succès relatif du Printemps arabe, la Tunisie tente de consolider sa démocratie naissante tout en faisant face à des menaces terroristes et à une économie fragilisée.
TUN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Tunisia]
- Political landscape: President Beji Caid Essebsi (Nidaa Tounes) and PM Habib Essid governing; only Arab Spring democracy surviving; Nobel Peace Prize to National Dialogue Quartet 2015.
- Key challenges: Terrorism (Sousse and Bardo museum attacks in 2015), youth unemployment (~30%), regional inequality (interior vs. coast), ISIS recruitment, Ben Guerdane border threat.
- Economic situation: Growth ~0.8%, tourism devastated by terrorist attacks, IMF program, unemployment high especially among educated youth; democratic transition economic costs.

LBY.name: State of Libya
LBY.description: État en déliquescence depuis la chute de Kadhafi, la Libye est déchirée entre gouvernements rivaux et milices concurrentes tout en restant une plaque tournante migratoire vers l'Europe.
LBY.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Libya]
- Political landscape: Two rival governments — UN-backed GNA (Tripoli) being formed under Fayez al-Sarraj, and House of Representatives (Tobruk) under Khalifa Haftar's influence; ISIS presence in Sirte.
- Key challenges: State collapse since 2011, civil war between rival factions, ISIS controlling Sirte, migrant trafficking hub for Mediterranean crossings, oil infrastructure damaged.
- Economic situation: Oil production crashed from 1.6M to ~400K barrels/day; economy in freefall; central bank reserves depleting; currency black market; hyperinflation in some areas.
- Regional tensions: UN brokering Libyan Political Agreement (December 2015); Egypt and UAE backing Haftar; Turkey and Qatar backing Tripoli factions; weapons flowing to Sahel.

KEN.name: Republic of Kenya
KEN.description: Hub économique et diplomatique de l'Afrique de l'Est, le Kenya combat le terrorisme d'Al-Shabaab tout en développant son économie numérique et ses services.
KEN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Kenya]
- Political landscape: President Uhuru Kenyatta (Jubilee Alliance); ICC charges dropped in 2014; governance concerns but democratic institutions functioning; opposition CORD active.
- Key challenges: Al-Shabaab terrorism (Garissa University attack April 2015, 148 dead), corruption, ethnic tensions, drought cycles, rapid urbanization, refugee hosting (Dadaab).
- Economic situation: East Africa's economic hub, ~5.7% growth, mobile money (M-Pesa) success story, Standard Gauge Railway under construction (Chinese-funded), tourism recovering.
- Regional tensions: Troops in Somalia (AMISOM, since 2011 against Al-Shabaab), hosting ~500,000 refugees (mostly Somali), Lake Turkana wind project, East African Community integration.

TZA.name: United Republic of Tanzania
TZA.description: Grande démocratie d'Afrique de l'Est riche en ressources naturelles et en patrimoine touristique, la Tanzanie sous Magufuli adopte un nationalisme économique affirmé.
TZA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Tanzania]
- Political landscape: President John Magufuli (CCM) newly inaugurated October 2015; 'The Bulldozer' reputation for anti-corruption drive; cutting government waste dramatically.
- Key challenges: Poverty despite growth, governance in Zanzibar (disputed election rerun), infrastructure deficit, wildlife poaching (elephants), natural gas development planning.
- Economic situation: Growth ~7%, agriculture (70% employment), gold mining, tourism (Serengeti, Kilimanjaro), offshore natural gas discoveries promising; port of Dar es Salaam regional gateway.

AGO.name: Republic of Angola
AGO.description: Économie pétrolière d'Afrique centrale et australe, l'Angola sous dos Santos maintient un régime autoritaire stable tout en s'appuyant massivement sur les investissements chinois.
AGO.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Angola]
- Political landscape: President Jose Eduardo dos Santos in power since 1979; MPLA one-party dominance; civil war ended 2002 but political freedoms limited.
- Key challenges: Oil price collapse devastating economy (oil = 95% exports), extreme inequality despite oil wealth, press freedom restrictions, corruption.
- Economic situation: GDP growth crashed from 4.8% to near-zero as oil revenues collapsed; kwanza devaluation; foreign reserves depleted; IMF assistance being considered.

GHA.name: Republic of Ghana
GHA.description: Modèle démocratique d'Afrique de l'Ouest, le Ghana est reconnu pour ses transitions pacifiques du pouvoir et sa stabilité relative au sein d'une région souvent turbulente.
GHA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Ghana]
- Political landscape: President John Mahama (NDC) facing December 2016 election; fiscal crisis and power outages ('dumsor') eroding support; opposition NPP strengthening.
- Key challenges: Power crisis (chronic electricity shortages), fiscal deficit, public debt rising sharply, corruption, illegal mining (galamsey), Ebola preparedness.
- Economic situation: Growth slowed to ~3.8% from double digits in 2011; IMF bailout program since 2015; cedi depreciating; oil production from Jubilee field helping; cocoa and gold exports.

CIV.name: Republic of Côte d'Ivoire
CIV.description: Premier producteur mondial de cacao et locomotive économique de l'Afrique de l'Ouest francophone, la Côte d'Ivoire se reconstruit après une décennie de crises politiques.
CIV.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Cote d'Ivoire]
- Political landscape: President Alassane Ouattara re-elected October 2015; post-civil war recovery since 2011; reconciliation process ongoing but incomplete.
- Key challenges: Post-conflict reconciliation (2010-2011 crisis), youth unemployment, cocoa sector governance, northern-southern divide, security sector reform.
- Economic situation: Fastest-growing economies in West Africa (~8.5% growth); world's largest cocoa producer; infrastructure investment program; Abidjan as regional commercial hub.
- Regional tensions: UN peacekeeping mission (UNOCI) drawing down; terrorist attack on Grand-Bassam beach resort will occur in March 2016; French military presence maintained.

SDN.name: Republic of Sudan
SDN.description: Régime sous sanctions internationales dirigé par Omar el-Béchir inculpé par la CPI, le Soudan cherche à sortir de son isolement tout en gérant de multiples conflits internes.
SDN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Sudan]
- Political landscape: President Omar al-Bashir in power since 1989 coup; ICC arrest warrant for Darfur genocide; authoritarian Islamist governance; April 2015 re-election boycotted.
- Key challenges: Darfur conflict continuing, South Kordofan and Blue Nile conflicts, Bashir ICC warrant, US sanctions, isolation, ethnic and religious tensions.
- Economic situation: Lost 75% of oil revenue when South Sudan seceded (2011); inflation high; gold mining partially compensating; Chinese investment in remaining oil sector.
- Regional tensions: Supporting Saudi-led coalition in Yemen; rapprochement with Gulf states; Nile water disputes with Egypt and Ethiopia; hosting Eritrean refugees.

SOM.name: Federal Republic of Somalia
SOM.description: L'un des États les plus fragiles au monde, la Somalie combat Al-Shabaab avec le soutien de l'AMISOM tout en tentant de reconstruire ses institutions après trois décennies de guerre civile.
SOM.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Somalia]
- Political landscape: President Hassan Sheikh Mohamud heading Federal Government with limited territorial control; clan politics dominant; Al-Shabaab insurgency ongoing.
- Key challenges: Al-Shabaab controlling large rural areas, state-building in failed state context, piracy declining but not eliminated, famine risk, displacement (~1.1M IDPs).
- Economic situation: Among world's poorest countries; livestock, remittances, and telecoms (mobile money) as economic base; diaspora remittances crucial (~$1.4B/year).
- Regional tensions: African Union peacekeeping force AMISOM (~22,000 troops) supporting government; Somaliland de facto independent; Puntland semi-autonomous; Kenya and Ethiopia military operations.

ZWE.name: Republic of Zimbabwe
ZWE.description: Sous l'emprise de Mugabe depuis l'indépendance, le Zimbabwe est en crise économique chronique et isolé de l'Occident tout en cultivant des liens avec la Chine et la Russie.
ZWE.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Zimbabwe]
- Political landscape: President Robert Mugabe (ZANU-PF) aged 91, in power since 1980; succession battle between VP Mnangagwa and wife Grace Mugabe; opposition MDC-T weakened.
- Key challenges: Mugabe succession crisis, cash shortages in dollarized economy, drought and food insecurity, land reform aftermath, international isolation, unemployment (~90% informal).
- Economic situation: Stagnant GDP (~1.1% growth), deflation, liquidity crisis (bond notes being considered), diamond revenues opaque, agriculture collapsed from land reform; Chinese loans.

ZMB.name: Republic of Zambia
ZMB.description: Économie zambienne fortement dépendante du cuivre, confrontée à une dette croissante envers la Chine et à des défis économiques liés à la chute des prix des matières premières.
ZMB.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Zambia]
- Political landscape: President Edgar Lungu (PF) in power after January 2015 special election following President Sata's death; election scheduled August 2016; opposition UPND competitive.
- Key challenges: Copper price collapse devastating economy, electricity crisis (drought reducing hydropower from Kariba Dam), kwacha depreciation, poverty, Chinese mining concerns.
- Economic situation: Copper-dependent economy in crisis; growth slowed to ~3%; kwacha lost 45% in 2015; IMF discussions; electricity rationing cutting production; Eurobond debt concerns.

CMR.name: Republic of Cameroon
CMR.description: Sous le règne quasi éternel de Paul Biya, le Cameroun est un pivot de l'Afrique centrale francophone combattant Boko Haram au nord tout en maintenant sa stabilité relative.
CMR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Cameroon]
- Political landscape: President Paul Biya in power since 1982; authoritarian governance; Anglophone minority grievances growing in northwest and southwest regions.
- Key challenges: Boko Haram attacks in Far North region, Anglophone crisis brewing, poverty, corruption, aging autocratic leadership, limited press freedom.
- Economic situation: Central Africa's largest economy, oil production declining, agriculture (cocoa, coffee) important, growth ~5.5%; planning for 2019 Africa Cup of Nations.
