- Regional tensions: Frontline state against Boko Haram with military operations in Far North; Central African refugee influx; maritime border dispute with Nigeria resolved at ICJ.

CHL.name: Republic of Chile
CHL.description: L'une des économies les plus stables et ouvertes d'Amérique latine, le Chili est un modèle de gestion macro-économique fondée sur l'exportation de cuivre.
CHL.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Chile]
- Political landscape: President Michelle Bachelet (PS) pushing education and tax reforms; approval ratings declining; corruption scandals (Caval affair involving her son).
- Key challenges: Education system reform demands, copper price decline, income inequality despite prosperity, Mapuche indigenous tensions in south, earthquake vulnerability.
- Economic situation: Copper price slump slowing growth to ~2.1%; inflation above target; peso weakening; Chile remains Latin America's most credit-worthy economy.
- Regional tensions: Maritime border dispute with Bolivia at ICJ; Pacific Alliance member; TPP signatory; stable relations with neighbors.

PER.name: Republic of Peru
PER.description: Économie émergente andine dont la croissance repose sur ses richesses minières, le Pérou maintient une politique étrangère modérée et des liens commerciaux forts avec la Chine.
PER.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Peru]
- Political landscape: President Ollanta Humala (Partido Nacionalista) completing term; April 2016 election upcoming with Keiko Fujimori as frontrunner; corruption investigations.
- Key challenges: Illegal mining and deforestation in Amazon, cocaine production, El Nino impact, inequality, informal economy (~70%), corruption.
- Economic situation: Growth slowed to ~3.3% from copper price decline; mining still key sector; stable macroeconomics; Pacific Alliance member; sol relatively stable.

ECU.name: Republic of Ecuador
ECU.description: Sous Rafael Correa, l'Équateur a adopté une politique étrangère indépendante anti-américaine, accordant notamment l'asile à Julian Assange à l'ambassade de Quito à Londres.
ECU.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Ecuador]
- Political landscape: President Rafael Correa (PAIS Alliance) in power since 2007; leftist agenda, citizens' revolution; constitutional amendment allowing indefinite re-election passed 2015.
- Key challenges: Oil price drop devastating economy (oil = 50% exports), dollarized economy limiting policy tools, press freedom concerns, Julian Assange in London embassy.
- Economic situation: GDP contracting as oil revenues collapse; dollarization preventing currency devaluation; Chinese loans filling financing gap; fiscal austerity needed.

URY.name: Oriental Republic of Uruguay
URY.description: Pays le plus stable et progressiste d'Amérique du Sud, l'Uruguay est pionnier sur les droits sociaux et maintient une démocratie robuste dans une région souvent agitée.
URY.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Uruguay]
- Political landscape: President Tabare Vazquez (Frente Amplio) in second non-consecutive term; progressive social policies (marijuana legalization, same-sex marriage).
- Key challenges: Economic slowdown from regional downturn (Argentina, Brazil), rising fiscal deficit, education reform needs, aging population relative to Latin America.
- Economic situation: High-income country for Latin America (~$15K GDP per capita), growth slowing to ~1%, strong institutions, low corruption, beef and soy exports, wind energy investment.

BOL.name: Plurinational State of Bolivia
BOL.description: Sous Evo Morales, première président indigène du pays, la Bolivie nationalise ses ressources en gaz et en lithium dans une logique anti-impérialiste et de redistribution sociale.
BOL.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Bolivia]
- Political landscape: President Evo Morales (MAS) in power since 2006; referendum on allowing fourth term scheduled for February 2016; indigenous rights agenda.
- Key challenges: Poverty despite improvements, coca/cocaine trade, infrastructure gaps, dependence on gas exports, water scarcity in some regions.
- Economic situation: Strong growth (~4.8%) driven by natural gas exports, significant poverty reduction under Morales, but commodities downturn threatening fiscal stability.
- Regional tensions: Landlocked; long-standing maritime dispute with Chile at ICJ; aligned with Venezuela and Cuba in ALBA bloc; tense relations with US after DEA expulsion.

PRY.name: Republic of Paraguay
PRY.description: Pays enclavé d'Amérique du Sud, le Paraguay est l'un des rares États reconnaissant Taiwan plutôt que la Chine populaire et repose sur une économie agro-pastorale.
PRY.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Paraguay]
- Political landscape: President Horacio Cartes (Colorado Party) in power since 2013; conservative businessman-politician; democratic governance but weak institutions.
- Key challenges: Inequality, corruption, EPP guerrilla group (small), deforestation in Chaco, soy monoculture, contraband/smuggling, indigenous rights.
- Economic situation: Growth ~3% (slowed from soy and hydroelectric sectors); world's fourth-largest soy exporter; Itaipu and Yacyreta hydroelectric dams; large informal economy.

PAN.name: Republic of Panama
PAN.description: Hub logistique et financier des Amériques grâce au Canal de Panama et à sa place financière, le Panama reçoit en 2016 une attention mondiale avec les Panama Papers.
PAN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Panama]
- Political landscape: President Juan Carlos Varela (Panamenista) governing; Panama Canal expansion nearing completion (inaugurated June 2016); democratic governance stable.
- Key challenges: Inequality despite strong growth, corruption, drug trafficking transit, Panama Papers leak (will break April 2016), Colon free zone decline.
- Economic situation: One of Latin America's fastest-growing economies (~5.8% growth); canal revenues, banking center, real estate boom; Tocumen airport expansion.

GTM.name: Republic of Guatemala
GTM.description: Plus grand pays d'Amérique centrale, le Guatemala fait face à une corruption endémique et est un carrefour de la migration irrégulière vers les États-Unis.
GTM.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Guatemala]
- Political landscape: President Jimmy Morales (FCN) just inaugurated January 14, 2016; comedian-turned-politician elected on anti-corruption wave after predecessor jailed.
- Key challenges: Corruption, drug trafficking, gang violence (one of world's highest homicide rates), poverty (60%), malnutrition especially among indigenous Maya, impunity.
- Economic situation: Central America's largest economy, ~4.1% growth, remittances from US (~10% GDP), coffee and textile exports; CICIG anti-corruption commission supported by UN.

CRI.name: Republic of Costa Rica
CRI.description: Unique démocratie d'Amérique centrale sans armée permanente, le Costa Rica est un modèle de développement durable et de stabilité politique dans une région instable.
CRI.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Costa Rica]
- Political landscape: President Luis Guillermo Solis (PAC) governing with minority in legislature; corruption investigations into predecessors; democratic tradition strong.
- Key challenges: Fiscal deficit growing, Cuban migrant crisis (stranded migrants on route to US), drug trafficking, inequality rising, infrastructure gaps.
- Economic situation: Stable middle-income economy, ~3.7% growth, ecotourism and tech (Intel) important, but fiscal deficit reaching 6% GDP; no military since 1948.

DOM.name: Dominican Republic
DOM.description: L'une des économies les plus dynamiques des Caraïbes grâce à son tourisme de masse, la République Dominicaine entretient des relations complexes avec Haïti avec lequel elle partage l'île d'Hispaniola.
DOM.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Dominican Republic]
- Political landscape: President Danilo Medina (PLD) preparing for May 2016 re-election after constitutional amendment; popular for economic management.
- Key challenges: Haitian immigration tensions, statelessness crisis affecting Dominican-born Haitians, corruption, inequality, drug trafficking transit.
- Economic situation: Fastest-growing economy in Latin America/Caribbean (~7% growth), tourism and remittances driving growth; mining (Pueblo Viejo gold mine) contributing.

TWN.name: Republic of China (Taiwan)
TWN.description: Démocratie insulaire revendiquée par la Chine populaire, Taïwan est un géant mondial des semi-conducteurs dont le statut international reste ambigu et source de tensions majeures.
TWN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Taiwan]
- Political landscape: Tsai Ing-wen (DPP) won presidential election January 16, 2016 in landslide; first female president; Beijing concerned about independence-leaning DPP.
- Key challenges: Cross-strait relations uncertainty with DPP victory, economic slowdown, stagnant wages, housing costs, aging population, declining birth rate.
- Economic situation: Export-dependent economy (~$530B GDP), semiconductor industry (TSMC world leader), growth slowed to ~0.7%; trade with China dominant but diversification sought.
- Regional tensions: China demanding acknowledgment of '1992 Consensus'; US arms sales; South China Sea claims; Trans-Pacific Partnership ambitions.

BGD.name: People's Republic of Bangladesh
BGD.description: L'une des économies à croissance la plus rapide au monde grâce à son industrie textile, le Bangladesh est aussi l'un des pays les plus vulnérables aux effets du changement climatique.
BGD.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Bangladesh]
- Political landscape: PM Sheikh Hasina (Awami League) governing firmly after controversial 2014 elections boycotted by opposition; crackdown on Islamist groups and opposition BNP.
- Key challenges: Garment factory safety (post-Rana Plaza 2013), climate change vulnerability (sea level rise), overpopulation, Islamist extremism rising, Rohingya refugee influx.
- Economic situation: Strong GDP growth (~6.5%), garment exports driving economy (80% of exports), remittances important, improving human development indicators.
- Regional tensions: Rohingya crisis at Myanmar border, tensions with Myanmar, close ties with India, China investing in infrastructure.

MMR.name: Republic of the Union of Myanmar
MMR.leader: Htin Kyaw / Aung San Suu Kyi
MMR.description: En transition démocratique fragile après des décennies de junte militaire, le Myanmar d'Aung San Suu Kyi s'ouvre au monde tout en gérant la crise rohingya persistante.
MMR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Myanmar]
- Political landscape: Aung San Suu Kyi's NLD won landmark November 2015 elections in landslide; transitioning from military rule; military retains 25% of parliament and key ministries.
- Key challenges: Military still controls defense, border, and home affairs; Rohingya persecution in Rakhine State; ethnic armed conflicts in Kachin and Shan States; poverty.
- Economic situation: Rapid growth (~7%) as economy opens after decades of isolation; foreign investment surging; gas exports, garments, agriculture; infrastructure severely underdeveloped.
- Regional tensions: Rohingya crisis generating regional tensions; ethnic armed groups along Chinese and Thai borders; opium production; Chinese investment (Myitsone Dam suspended).

LKA.name: Democratic Socialist Republic of Sri Lanka
LKA.description: Île de l'océan Indien en reconstruction après la fin de la guerre civile tamoule, le Sri Lanka navigue entre l'influence indienne et chinoise dans un contexte de compétition géopolitique.
LKA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Sri Lanka]
- Political landscape: President Maithripala Sirisena and PM Ranil Wickremesinghe in uneasy coalition after defeating Rajapaksa in 2015; reconciliation process after civil war.
- Key challenges: Post-civil war reconciliation with Tamil minority, transitional justice, Chinese debt trap concerns (Hambantota port), political reform, religious extremism.
- Economic situation: Growth ~4.8%, tourism recovering, tea and garment exports, but debt burden growing; EU GSP+ trade preferences being pursued; Chinese infrastructure loans.

KHM.name: Kingdom of Cambodia
KHM.description: Sous la mainmise de Hun Sen depuis trois décennies, le Cambodge est l'allié le plus proche de la Chine au sein de l'ASEAN et dépend largement des investissements de Pékin.
KHM.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Cambodia]
- Political landscape: PM Hun Sen (CPP) in power since 1985; crackdown on opposition CNRP intensifying; opposition leader Sam Rainsy in self-imposed exile.
- Key challenges: Authoritarian backsliding, land grabbing, deforestation, garment worker rights, Khmer Rouge tribunal ongoing, extreme poverty in rural areas.
- Economic situation: Growth ~7%, garment manufacturing and tourism driving economy; rice exports growing; Chinese investment and aid increasing; heavy dependence on foreign aid.

JOR.name: Hashemite Kingdom of Jordan
JOR.description: Monarchie hachémite stable et modérée au cœur du Moyen-Orient, la Jordanie accueille plus d'un million de réfugiés syriens et joue un rôle clé dans les efforts de paix régionaux.
JOR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Jordan]
- Political landscape: King Abdullah II ruling with appointed government; PM Abdullah Ensour; limited political reforms since Arab Spring; key Western ally in region.
- Key challenges: Hosting ~650,000 Syrian refugees (1.3M total Syrians, huge burden for 9.5M population), water scarcity, unemployment, energy import dependence, ISIS threat.
- Economic situation: Limited natural resources, growth ~2.4%, heavily dependent on foreign aid (especially US, Gulf), tourism hurt by regional instability; hosting Zaatari refugee camp.
- Regional tensions: Border with Syria and Iraq; participating in anti-ISIS coalition; managing refugee burden; Palestine issue; close military cooperation with US.

LBN.name: Lebanese Republic
LBN.description: Mosaic confessionnelle en crise institutionnelle permanente, le Liban accueille aussi plus d'un million de réfugiés syriens et voit son équilibre menacé par les tensions régionales.
LBN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Lebanon]
- Political landscape: Presidential vacuum since May 2014 (no agreement between March 14 and March 8 blocs); PM Tammam Salam heading caretaker government; parliament extending own mandate.
- Key challenges: Syrian refugee crisis (~1.5M refugees for population of 4.5M), political paralysis, Hezbollah's dual role (party and militia), garbage crisis ('You Stink' movement), sectarian tensions.
- Economic situation: Growth near zero, tourism devastated, banking sector under pressure, public debt ~140% GDP; infrastructure crumbling; Syrian refugees straining services.
- Regional tensions: Hezbollah fighting in Syria for Assad; spillover bombings and kidnappings; Saudi-Iran rivalry playing out through Lebanese politics; Sunni radicalization in Tripoli/Sidon.

KWT.name: State of Kuwait
KWT.description: Petite monarchie pétrolière profondément dépendante de la protection américaine depuis l'invasion irakienne de 1990, le Koweït est l'un des États du Golfe les plus riches par habitant.
KWT.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Kuwait]
- Political landscape: Emir Sabah Al-Ahmad Al-Jaber Al-Sabah ruling; relatively open parliament for Gulf but recurring government-parliament tensions; Bidun stateless issue.
- Key challenges: Oil price crash straining generous welfare state, diversification needed, Bidun (stateless residents) rights, ISIS attack on Shia mosque in June 2015, labor rights.
- Economic situation: Oil-rich with massive sovereign wealth fund (KIA, ~$600B); budget moving into deficit from oil crash; 6% of world's proven oil reserves.

OMN.name: Sultanate of Oman
OMN.description: Sultanat discret du Golfe jouant un rôle de médiateur entre l'Iran et l'Occident, Oman sous le sultan Qaboûs maintient des relations avec tous les acteurs de la région.
OMN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Oman]
- Political landscape: Sultan Qaboos bin Said in power since 1970; aging and in poor health (cancer treatment in Germany 2014-2015); succession planning opaque.
- Key challenges: Sultan's health and unclear succession, oil price drop stressing budget, diversification need, youth unemployment, water scarcity.
- Economic situation: Oil-dependent but more diversified than Gulf peers; budget deficit from oil crash; tourism and logistics (Duqm port) being developed; moderate debt levels.

YEM.name: Republic of Yemen
YEM.description: Théâtre d'une guerre civile dévastatrice depuis 2015, le Yémen est le terrain d'un conflit par procuration entre l'Arabie Saoudite et l'Iran, provoquant une catastrophe humanitaire.
YEM.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Yemen]
- Political landscape: President Hadi in exile (Riyadh); Houthi rebels and Saleh forces controlling Sanaa; country split between multiple factions; UN peace talks attempted.
- Key challenges: Catastrophic civil war (Saudi-led coalition bombing since March 2015), humanitarian crisis (21M need aid, 80% population), AQAP and ISIS exploiting chaos, cholera risk.
- Economic situation: Economy destroyed by war; infrastructure devastated; famine conditions in multiple areas; ports blockaded limiting imports; pre-war already Arab world's poorest.
- Regional tensions: Saudi-led coalition (including UAE, Bahrain, Sudan) fighting Houthi-Saleh alliance; Iran accused of supporting Houthis; AQAP controlling territory in south; Bab el-Mandeb strait security.

AFG.name: Islamic Republic of Afghanistan
AFG.description: État en reconstruction permanente sous tutelle internationale, l'Afghanistan combat les Taliban avec le soutien de l'OTAN tout en restant rongé par la corruption et l'instabilité.
AFG.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Afghanistan]
- Political landscape: President Ashraf Ghani in fragile national unity government with CEO Abdullah Abdullah; Taliban insurgency intensifying after NATO combat mission ended 2014.
- Key challenges: Taliban controlling or contesting large swathes of territory, ISIS-Khorasan emerging, opium production at record levels, weak governance and corruption.
- Regional tensions: Pakistan border tensions, Taliban safe havens in Pakistan, India-Pakistan rivalry playing out in Afghanistan, reduced US/NATO troop presence (~13,000 remaining).
- Economic situation: One of the world's poorest countries, heavily aid-dependent, GDP ~$20B, unemployment widespread, capital flight as foreign forces withdraw.

UZB.name: Republic of Uzbekistan
UZB.description: Pays le plus peuplé d'Asie centrale sous le pouvoir absolu de Karimov, l'Ouzbékistan est une charnière régionale stratégique entre Russie, Chine et Afghanistan.
UZB.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Uzbekistan]
- Political landscape: President Islam Karimov ruling since 1989; Central Asia's most authoritarian state; no genuine elections; forced labor in cotton harvest.
- Key challenges: Authoritarian repression, forced labor (cotton), Aral Sea environmental disaster, Islamist extremism concerns (IMU), economic isolation, border tensions.
- Economic situation: Central Asia's most populous country (~31M); gold and cotton exports; state-controlled economy; officially ~8% growth but data unreliable; currency black market.

MNG.name: Mongolia
MNG.description: Démocratie enclavée entre Russie et Chine, la Mongolie pratique une politique de 'troisième voisin' en cultivant des liens avec les démocraties occidentales pour compenser sa dépendance géographique.
MNG.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Mongolia]
- Political landscape: President Tsakhiagiin Elbegdorj (DP) and PM Chimed Saikhanbileg; democracy in challenging neighborhood; 'third neighbor' policy balancing Russia and China.
- Key challenges: Mining-dependent economy in commodity downturn, Oyu Tolgoi copper/gold mine disputes with Rio Tinto, poverty, harsh climate, air pollution in Ulaanbaatar.
- Economic situation: Growth crashed from 17% (2011) to ~2.4% as commodity prices fell; fiscal deficit widening; debt rising; IMF assistance being considered.

NZL.name: New Zealand
NZL.description: Démocratie pacifique du Pacifique Sud, la Nouvelle-Zélande est connue pour sa politique anti-nucléaire assumée et son modèle progressiste de gouvernance biculturelle maorie-pakeha.
NZL.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — New Zealand]
- Political landscape: PM John Key (National) popular; third term; TPP signatory; considering flag change referendum in 2016; strong governance indicators.
- Key challenges: Housing affordability crisis (especially Auckland), dairy price slump, Christchurch earthquake rebuild ongoing, Maori inequality, distance from markets.
- Economic situation: Growth ~3.4%, dairy and tourism pillars, net migration at record highs driving growth; New Zealand dollar softening with dairy prices.

HTI.name: Haiti
HTI.description: Pays le plus pauvre des Amériques, Haïti reste marqué par le séisme de 2010 et une instabilité politique chronique malgré une reconstruction partielle.
HTI.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Haiti]
- Political landscape: Electoral crisis; disputed October 2015 election results; President Michel Martelly's term expiring February 2016 without elected successor; UN stabilization mission MINUSTAH present.
- Key challenges: Poorest country in Western Hemisphere, 2010 earthquake recovery still incomplete, cholera epidemic (UN-linked), deforestation, gang violence, political instability.
- Economic situation: GDP per capita under $800, heavily dependent on remittances and international aid; garment assembly for US market; chronic food insecurity.

JAM.name: Jamaica
JAM.description: Île des Caraïbes anglophones, la Jamaïque est membre du Commonwealth et dépend largement du tourisme et des transferts de la diaspora.
JAM.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Jamaica]
- Political landscape: PM Portia Simpson-Miller (PNP) heading into February 2016 election; democratic governance stable; IMF program shaping economic policy.
- Key challenges: High crime and gang violence, public debt (~125% GDP), poverty, brain drain (emigration), vulnerability to hurricanes, marijuana policy reform.
- Economic situation: Slow growth (~1%), IMF Extended Fund Facility driving fiscal discipline, tourism and remittances as economic pillars; bauxite/alumina exports declining.

TTO.name: Trinidad and Tobago
TTO.description: Île la plus prospère des Caraïbes grâce à ses ressources en pétrole et gaz, Trinité-et-Tobago est le leader économique de la CARICOM mais subit la chute des cours en 2016.
TTO.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Trinidad and Tobago]
- Political landscape: PM Keith Rowley (PNM) took office September 2015; energy-dependent economy; democratic governance stable; dealing with ISIS recruitment concerns.
- Key challenges: Oil and gas price decline, crime and gang violence, ISIS recruitment (highest per capita Caribbean contributor to foreign fighters), diversification needed.
- Economic situation: Recession from energy price collapse; gas (LNG) and petrochemicals dominant; budget deficit widening; previously high-income Caribbean economy facing adjustment.

BHS.name: Bahamas
BHS.description: Archipel prospère vivant principalement du tourisme et de la finance offshore, les Bahamas entretiennent des liens très étroits avec les États-Unis.
BHS.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Bahamas]
- Political landscape: PM Perry Christie (PLP); archipelagic nation heavily dependent on tourism and financial services; democratic governance stable.
- Key challenges: Drug trafficking transit route, illegal immigration from Haiti, vulnerability to hurricanes, economic dependence on US tourism, high cost of living.
- Economic situation: Upper-middle-income country, GDP growth ~1%, tourism (60% GDP) and offshore banking driving economy; national debt rising.

BLZ.name: Belize
BLZ.description: Seul pays anglophone d'Amérique centrale, le Belize entretient un vieux différend territorial avec le Guatemala et dépend fortement du tourisme et de l'agriculture.
BLZ.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Belize]
- Political landscape: PM Dean Barrow (UDP) governing; small Central American nation; democratic institutions functioning; territorial dispute with Guatemala ongoing.
- Key challenges: Drug trafficking transit point, high violent crime rate, poverty, vulnerability to hurricanes, limited economic diversification, debt burden.
- Economic situation: Small economy dependent on tourism, agriculture (sugar, citrus, bananas), and services; GDP growth ~1%; public debt above 75% GDP.

GUY.name: Guyana
GUY.description: Seul pays anglophone d'Amérique du Sud, le Guyana est en plein essor grâce à la découverte de vastes réserves pétrolières offshore par ExxonMobil en 2015.
GUY.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Guyana]
- Political landscape: President David Granger (APNU-AFC) took office May 2015, ending 23 years of PPP rule; ethnic politics (Afro-Guyanese vs Indo-Guyanese) still dominant.
- Key challenges: Poverty, emigration (more Guyanese abroad than in country), ethnic tensions, infrastructure deficit, deforestation, drug trafficking transit.
- Economic situation: Small economy dependent on sugar, rice, gold, and bauxite; ExxonMobil discovered massive offshore oil reserves (Liza field, 2015) — transformative potential.

SUR.name: Suriname
SUR.description: Ancienne colonie néerlandaise riche en pétrole, or et bauxite, le Suriname traverse une grave crise économique en 2016 sous la présidence controversée de Bouterse.
SUR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Suriname]
- Political landscape: President Desi Bouterse (NDP) in second term; convicted drug trafficker and former military dictator; November 2015 military tribunal convicted him of 1982 murders.
- Key challenges: Commodity dependence (gold, oil, bauxite), drug trafficking, deforestation, ethnic diversity management, small population (~550K), judicial independence.
- Economic situation: Recession from commodity price declines; gold and oil revenues falling; suriname dollar devaluing; fiscal crisis; Dutch colonial legacy infrastructure.

HND.name: Honduras
HND.description: Le Honduras est l'un des pays les plus pauvres d'Amérique latine, confronté à la violence des gangs et à la corruption, très dépendant de l'aide américaine.
HND.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Honduras]
- Political landscape: President Juan Orlando Hernandez (National Party); controversial re-election bid despite constitutional single-term limit; Berta Caceres environmental activism.
- Key challenges: Among world's highest homicide rates, gang violence (MS-13, Barrio 18), drug trafficking, poverty (~65%), corruption, mass emigration to US.
- Economic situation: Low-income country, ~3.6% growth, dependent on remittances (18% GDP) and maquila exports; coffee important; US aid tied to migration cooperation.

SLV.name: El Salvador
SLV.description: Le pays le plus densément peuplé d'Amérique centrale, El Salvador dollarisé souffre de la violence des gangs MS-13 et Barrio 18 malgré une économie en légère croissance.
SLV.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — El Salvador]
- Political landscape: President Salvador Sanchez Ceren (FMLN) governing; former guerrilla commander; gang truce collapsed; Supreme Court declared gangs terrorist organizations.
- Key challenges: World's highest homicide rate (103 per 100,000 in 2015), MS-13 and Barrio 18 gang warfare, poverty, emigration to US, remittance dependence.
- Economic situation: Dollarized economy, slow growth (~2.5%), remittances (17% GDP), maquila sector, coffee; smallest and most densely populated Central American country.

NIC.name: Nicaragua
NIC.description: Gouverné par Daniel Ortega depuis 2007, le Nicaragua s'appuie sur le Venezuela pour son pétrole subventionné et maintient des liens étroits avec Cuba et la Russie.
NIC.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Nicaragua]
- Political landscape: President Daniel Ortega (FSLN) consolidating power; Supreme Court removed opposition leaders; wife Rosario Murillo as de facto co-ruler; 2016 re-election planned.
- Key challenges: Democratic backsliding, poverty, corruption, interoceanic canal project (Chinese-backed, controversial), deforestation, emigration.
- Economic situation: Growth ~4.9% (Central America's strongest), Venezuelan oil subsidies (Petrocaribe), garment and coffee exports; second-poorest in Western Hemisphere.

SEN.name: Senegal
SEN.description: L'une des démocraties les plus stables d'Afrique de l'Ouest, le Sénégal est un partenaire clé de la France et un pôle régional de stabilité face au jihadisme sahélien.
SEN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Senegal]
- Political landscape: President Macky Sall (APR) consolidating democratic governance; constitutional referendum planned (March 2016) to reduce presidential term to 5 years.
- Key challenges: Casamance separatist conflict (low-level), youth unemployment, energy costs, fishing sector decline (foreign overfishing), poverty, Ebola preparedness.
- Economic situation: Growth ~6.5%, Plan Senegal Emergent driving investment, offshore oil and gas discoveries (Sangomar field) promising future revenues; tourism and agriculture important.

MLI.name: Mali
MLI.description: Depuis le coup d'État de 2012 et l'intervention française Serval/Barkhane, le Mali lutte contre les groupes jihadistes dans le Sahel, son nord restant très instable.
MLI.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Mali]
- Political landscape: President Ibrahim Boubacar Keita governing after 2013 French intervention restored order; peace agreement with northern rebel groups signed June 2015.
- Key challenges: Jihadist insurgency in north (AQIM, Ansar Dine, MUJAO), Tuareg separatism, implementation of Algiers peace accord, extreme poverty, food insecurity.
- Economic situation: Low-income country, ~6% growth driven by gold mining and agriculture (cotton); heavily aid-dependent; French military and UN peacekeeping (MINUSMA) present.
- Regional tensions: Key Sahel security concern; French Operation Barkhane headquartered in Bamako; UN peacekeeping mission MINUSMA is the deadliest active mission; hotel attack in Bamako November 2015.

BFA.name: Burkina Faso
BFA.description: Roch Marc Christian Kaboré est élu en 2015, marquant la première alternance pacifique depuis l'indépendance. Le pays fait face à une menace jihadiste croissante aux frontières du Mali.
BFA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Burkina Faso]
- Political landscape: President Roch Marc Christian Kabore just elected November 2015 in first democratic transition after October 2014 popular uprising ousted Blaise Compaore.
- Key challenges: Terrorism from Sahel jihadist groups (AQIM, Ansar Dine), extreme poverty, food insecurity, gold mining governance; hotel attack in Ouagadougou January 15, 2016.
- Economic situation: Low-income country with ~4% growth; gold and cotton main exports; heavily aid-dependent; one of the youngest populations in the world.
- Regional tensions: Jihadist threat spreading from Mali; part of G5 Sahel security cooperation; French military presence (Operation Barkhane).

NER.name: Niger
NER.description: Parmi les pays les plus pauvres du monde, le Niger est néanmoins un partenaire stratégique de l'Occident face à Boko Haram et dispose d'importantes ressources en uranium exploitées par Areva.
NER.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Niger]
- Political landscape: President Mahamadou Issoufou seeking re-election February 2016; opposition leader Hama Amadou arrested; democratic governance fragile.
- Key challenges: World's highest fertility rate (~7.6 children/woman), extreme poverty, Boko Haram attacks in southeast (Diffa region), food insecurity, uranium price decline.
- Economic situation: One of world's least developed countries; uranium mining (Areva/Orano), agriculture, livestock; growth ~3.5%; French military presence.

TCD.name: Chad
TCD.description: Allié militaire clé de la France au Sahel, le Tchad d'Idriss Déby joue un rôle central dans la lutte contre Boko Haram et les groupes armés de la région, malgré une gouvernance autoritaire.
TCD.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Chad]
- Political landscape: President Idriss Deby in power since 1990; authoritarian rule; presidential election April 2016; key Western counterterrorism ally despite poor governance.
- Key challenges: Boko Haram attacks, poverty, landlocked, oil dependence, food insecurity, Lake Chad shrinking, authoritarian governance, humanitarian crises from neighbors.
- Economic situation: Oil revenues devastated by price crash; budget crisis; French military hub (Operation Barkhane); among world's least developed countries.
- Regional tensions: Chadian forces fighting Boko Haram in Nigeria and Niger; contributing to UN peacekeeping in Mali; hosting refugees from CAR, Nigeria, Sudan.

GIN.name: Guinea
GIN.description: La Guinée possède les plus grandes réserves de bauxite au monde mais reste très pauvre. Alpha Condé gouverne dans un contexte de tensions ethniques post-élection.
GIN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Guinea]
- Political landscape: President Alpha Conde re-elected October 2015 amid opposition protests; recovering from Ebola epidemic that killed ~2,500 Guineans.
- Key challenges: Ebola aftermath (declared free in December 2015), extreme poverty, ethnic political tensions, mining governance, weak infrastructure.
- Economic situation: Rich in bauxite (world's largest reserves) and iron ore but extremely poor; growth recovering to ~3.5% post-Ebola; Simandou iron ore project stalled.

SLE.name: Sierra Leone
SLE.description: Encore en reconstruction après la guerre civile et l'épidémie d'Ebola de 2014-2016, la Sierra Leone voit son PIB s'effondrer de 21% en 2015 avec la chute des cours du minerai de fer.
SLE.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Sierra Leone]
- Political landscape: President Ernest Bai Koroma (APC) managing post-Ebola recovery; democratic gains since civil war (ended 2002); governance challenges.
- Key challenges: Ebola aftermath (3,955 deaths), health system devastated, extreme poverty, infrastructure gaps, youth unemployment, iron ore price collapse.
- Economic situation: Economy contracted ~21% in 2015 due to Ebola and iron ore price crash; mining (diamonds, iron ore, rutile), agriculture; heavily aid-dependent.

LBR.name: Liberia
LBR.description: Ellen Johnson Sirleaf, première femme élue chef d'État en Afrique, dirige un Liberia encore fragilisé par la guerre civile et les séquelles de l'épidémie d'Ebola.
LBR.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Liberia]
- Political landscape: President Ellen Johnson Sirleaf (UP) in second term; post-Ebola recovery; UNMIL peacekeeping drawing down ahead of 2017 elections.
- Key challenges: Ebola aftermath (4,800+ deaths, 2014-2015), rebuilding health system, poverty, unemployment, corruption, infrastructure devastated by civil wars.
- Economic situation: Economy shrank ~0.7% due to Ebola and commodity price declines; iron ore and rubber exports struggling; heavily aid-dependent; development indicators among world's lowest.

GNB.name: Guinea-Bissau
GNB.description: La Guinée-Bissau est l'un des États les plus fragiles au monde, régulièrement secoué par des coups d'État et utilisé comme plaque tournante du trafic de cocaïne.
GNB.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Guinea-Bissau]
- Political landscape: President Jose Mario Vaz and PM Carlos Correia in power but political instability chronic; military has staged multiple coups since independence.
- Key challenges: Drug trafficking hub (Latin American cocaine transits through), political instability, extreme poverty, military interference in politics, cashew nut monoculture.
- Economic situation: One of world's poorest countries, cashew nuts are primary export, heavily aid-dependent, limited infrastructure outside Bissau.

GMB.name: Gambia
GMB.description: Yahya Jammeh dirige la Gambie d'une main de fer depuis 1994, se distinguant par des déclarations extrémistes et une rupture progressive avec les organisations régionales.
GMB.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Gambia]
- Political landscape: President Yahya Jammeh in power since 1994 coup; increasingly erratic and authoritarian; declared Gambia an Islamic republic in 2015.
- Key challenges: Authoritarian rule, human rights abuses, press freedom eliminated, poverty, youth emigration (many among Mediterranean boat migrants), HIV/AIDS.
- Economic situation: Tiny economy dependent on tourism, groundnut exports, and remittances; one of Africa's smallest countries; re-export trade important.

MRT.name: Mauritania
MRT.description: La Mauritanie a réussi à contenir la menace djihadiste grâce à une politique de coopération sécuritaire avec la France et les États-Unis, sous la présidence autoritaire d'Abdel Aziz.
MRT.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Mauritania]
- Political landscape: President Mohamed Ould Abdel Aziz, former coup leader turned elected president; authoritarian governance; slavery legacy and racial tensions.
- Key challenges: Slavery persists despite legal prohibition, extreme poverty, desertification, Sahelian security threats, ethnic tensions (Arab-Berber vs. Black African).
- Economic situation: Low-income country dependent on iron ore, fishing, and livestock; mining revenues declining with commodity prices; offshore gas potential.

BEN.name: Benin
BEN.description: Le Bénin est l'une des démocraties les plus stables d'Afrique de l'Ouest, jouant un rôle de hub commercial entre le Nigeria et les pays sahéliens enclavés.
BEN.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Benin]
- Political landscape: President Thomas Boni Yayi completing second term; presidential election scheduled for March 2016; democratic tradition relatively strong for region.
- Key challenges: Poverty (one of world's least developed countries), infrastructure deficit, cotton-dependent agriculture vulnerable to commodity prices, corruption.
- Economic situation: GDP growth ~5%, heavily dependent on trade with Nigeria and cotton exports; port of Cotonou important for regional trade; informal economy dominant.

TGO.name: Togo
TGO.description: Faure Gnassingbé dirige le Togo depuis 2005, perpétuant la dynastie familiale. Le port de Lomé est un hub régional important pour les pays enclavés du Sahel.
TGO.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Togo]
- Political landscape: President Faure Gnassingbe in power since 2005, continuing father's dynasty (in power since 1967); contested April 2015 re-election; opposition protests.
- Key challenges: Political dynasty, poverty, limited infrastructure, phosphate mining governance, constitutional reform demands (term limits), youth unemployment.
- Economic situation: Low-income country, growth ~5.3%, phosphate mining, port of Lome as regional trade hub, agriculture dominant; West African franc zone member.

COD.name: Democratic Republic of the Congo
COD.description: La RDC est le pays le plus riche en ressources naturelles d'Afrique mais l'un des plus pauvres. L'Est du pays reste en proie à des groupes armés malgré la présence de la MONUSCO.
COD.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Democratic Republic of the Congo]
- Political landscape: President Joseph Kabila approaching constitutional two-term limit (2016); widespread fears of attempt to stay in power; opposition mobilizing.
- Key challenges: Eastern Congo armed groups (M23 defeated but ADF, FDLR active), Kabila third-term crisis, massive humanitarian needs, Ebola preparedness, mineral exploitation.
- Regional tensions: UN peacekeeping mission MONUSCO (~20,000 troops, largest in world); tensions with Rwanda and Uganda over eastern Congo; Burundi crisis spillover.
- Economic situation: Rich in minerals (cobalt, coltan, copper, diamonds) but one of world's poorest countries; growth ~6.9%; Chinese mining investments; infrastructure devastated.

COG.name: Republic of the Congo
COG.description: Denis Sassou Nguesso, au pouvoir depuis 1997, dirige le Congo-Brazzaville avec une économie dominée par le pétrole et de forts liens avec la France et la Chine.
COG.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Republic of the Congo]
- Political landscape: President Denis Sassou Nguesso engineered constitutional referendum in October 2015 to remove term limits; planning re-election; opposition crackdown.
- Key challenges: Oil dependence (90% exports), post-referendum tensions, poverty despite oil wealth, lack of diversification, youth unemployment.
- Economic situation: Oil-dependent economy hit by price crash; growth slowing sharply; Brazzaville arms depot explosion in March 2012 reconstruction ongoing; debt rising.

CAF.name: Central African Republic
CAF.description: La RCA est l'un des États les plus fragiles du monde, déchiré par les milices Séléka et Anti-Balaka depuis 2013, sous intervention de l'ONU (MINUSCA) et de la France (Sangaris).
CAF.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Central African Republic]
- Political landscape: Transitional government after 2013 Seleka coup and anti-Balaka reprisals; presidential elections held December 2015/February 2016 runoff pending.
- Key challenges: Ongoing sectarian violence between Muslim Seleka and Christian anti-Balaka militias, mass displacement (~1M), state collapse outside capital, humanitarian crisis.
- Regional tensions: UN peacekeeping mission MINUSCA deployed (~12,000 troops); French forces (Sangaris) withdrawing; regional spillover of conflict; natural resources fueling conflict.
- Economic situation: One of world's poorest countries, GDP under $2B, diamond and timber resources exploited by armed groups, infrastructure virtually nonexistent outside Bangui.

GAB.name: Gabon
GAB.description: Le Gabon est un pays pétrolier relativement prospère mais inégalitaire, gouverné par Ali Bongo dans la continuité de son père, étroit allié de Paris.
GAB.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Gabon]
- Political landscape: President Ali Bongo Ondimba succeeding his father who ruled 42 years; presidential election upcoming August 2016; opposition organizing.
- Key challenges: Oil dependence, youth unemployment despite oil wealth, inequality, governance concerns, diversification need, declining oil production.
- Economic situation: Upper-middle-income by African standards but highly unequal; oil revenues declining; timber and manganese also important; growth slowing to ~4%.

GNQ.name: Equatorial Guinea
GNQ.description: Obiang gouverne depuis 1979 dans une dictature pétrolière parmi les plus corrompues du monde, malgré un PIB par habitant élevé dû aux revenus pétroliers.
GNQ.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Equatorial Guinea]
- Political landscape: President Teodoro Obiang Nguema in power since 1979 (Africa's longest-serving leader); authoritarian rule, family dynasty, sham elections.
- Key challenges: Extreme inequality despite oil wealth, oil production declining, no political freedom, corruption (Obiang family wealth), human rights abuses.
- Economic situation: High GDP per capita from oil but vast majority live in poverty; oil production past peak; attempting to host international events for legitimacy.

UGA.name: Uganda
UGA.description: Museveni dirige l'Ouganda depuis 1986, maintenant une stabilité relative dans une région tumultueuse et jouant un rôle actif dans les crises en RDC et au Soudan du Sud.
UGA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Uganda]
- Political landscape: President Yoweri Museveni (NRM) in power since 1986; February 2016 elections upcoming; opposition leader Kizza Besigye repeatedly arrested; authoritarian drift.
- Key challenges: Museveni's 30-year rule, term limit removal, anti-homosexuality legislation controversy, population boom (~3.2% growth rate), Lord's Resistance Army remnants.
- Economic situation: Growth ~5%, oil production expected to start (Albertine Graben), agriculture dominant, hosting ~500,000 refugees (South Sudan, Burundi, DRC); aid-dependent.

RWA.name: Rwanda
RWA.description: Sous Paul Kagame, le Rwanda connaît une croissance économique remarquable et une reconstruction post-génocide impressionnante, mais au prix d'un régime très contrôlé des libertés.
RWA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Rwanda]
- Political landscape: President Paul Kagame (RPF) in power since 2000; December 2015 referendum approved constitutional changes allowing him to potentially rule until 2034.
- Key challenges: Authoritarian governance despite development success, press freedom absent, ethnic reconciliation (Hutu-Tutsi), regional meddling accusations, limited political space.
- Economic situation: Strong growth (~6.9%), 'Africa's Singapore' aspirations, services and agriculture driving economy, aid-dependent but efficient use of resources; Kigali clean city model.

BDI.name: Burundi
BDI.description: Le Burundi est plongé dans une grave crise depuis 2015, quand Pierre Nkurunziza a briguéun troisième mandat inconstitutionnel, provoquant violences et exodes massifs.
BDI.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Burundi]
- Political landscape: President Pierre Nkurunziza seized controversial third term in July 2015 despite constitutional limits; failed coup attempt; mass violence and repression.
- Key challenges: Political crisis triggering humanitarian emergency, over 200,000 refugees fled, extrajudicial killings, risk of ethnic violence (Hutu-Tutsi), media shut down.
- Regional tensions: Tensions with Rwanda (accused of supporting rebels), East African Community mediation failing, AU peacekeeping force rejected by Nkurunziza.
- Economic situation: One of the world's poorest countries, aid-dependent, economic collapse as donors suspend aid over political crisis.

SSD.name: South Sudan
SSD.description: Indépendant depuis 2011, le Soudan du Sud est ravagé par une guerre civile entre les forces de Salva Kiir et celles de Riek Machar depuis 2013, causant une catastrophe humanitaire.
SSD.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — South Sudan]
- Political landscape: President Salva Kiir and VP Riek Machar in nominal unity government under August 2015 peace agreement; deep mistrust; ethnic Dinka-Nuer divide.
- Key challenges: Civil war since December 2013 (tens of thousands killed, 2.2M displaced), famine risk, ethnic violence, oil production crashed, state failure.
- Economic situation: World's newest country (2011) in economic collapse; oil production (sole revenue source) halved by conflict; hyperinflation; 85% living below poverty line.
- Regional tensions: UN peacekeeping mission UNMISS deployed; IGAD mediating; refugees in Uganda, Sudan, Ethiopia, Kenya; oil transit dependence on Sudan.

ERI.name: Eritrea
ERI.description: L'Érythrée d'Isaias Afwerki est l'un des pays les plus fermés au monde, soumis à un service national quasi illimité et en état de guerre froide permanent avec l'Éthiopie.
ERI.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Eritrea]
- Political landscape: President Isaias Afwerki ruling since independence (1993) with no elections, no constitution implemented, no free press; often called 'Africa's North Korea.'
- Key challenges: Indefinite military conscription driving mass emigration (one of top sources of Mediterranean migrants), extreme repression, poverty, isolation.
- Economic situation: One of the poorest countries; mining (gold, copper) providing some revenue; remittances from diaspora crucial; UN Commission of Inquiry found crimes against humanity.
- Regional tensions: Unresolved border conflict with Ethiopia; tense relations with Djibouti; UN sanctions for alleged support to Somali extremists; thousands fleeing monthly.

DJI.name: Djibouti
DJI.description: Djibouti héberge des bases militaires françaises, américaines, japonaises et chinoises, tirant parti de sa position stratégique sur le détroit de Bab-el-Mandeb.
DJI.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Djibouti]
- Political landscape: President Ismail Omar Guelleh in power since 1999; authoritarian rule; strategic location at Bab el-Mandeb Strait attracting foreign military bases.
- Key challenges: Extreme poverty, water scarcity, limited arable land, youth unemployment, one-party dominance, press freedom restricted.
- Economic situation: Strategic rent economy from foreign military bases; US (Camp Lemonnier), France, Japan bases present; China constructing first overseas base; port expansion underway.
- Regional tensions: Ethiopia-Djibouti railway under construction; monitoring Yemen conflict across strait; hosting refugees from Somalia and Eritrea.

MOZ.name: Mozambique
MOZ.description: Le Mozambique fait face à une grave crise de la dette cachée découverte en 2016 (tuna bonds), mais ses réserves de gaz naturel dans le bassin du Rovuma suscitent les convoitises.
MOZ.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Mozambique]
- Political landscape: President Filipe Nyusi (Frelimo) in office since January 2015; hidden debt scandal about to emerge; Renamo opposition threatening renewed conflict.
- Key challenges: Hidden $2B in undisclosed government debt about to be revealed, Renamo armed opposition, poverty (~46%), natural gas development delays, corruption.
- Economic situation: Growth ~6.6% but built on unsustainable borrowing; massive natural gas reserves (Rovuma basin) attracting investment; coal exports; agricultural base.
- Regional tensions: Renamo leader Afonso Dhlakama in bush threatening conflict; government forces clashing with Renamo militias; Southern African development partner.

MDG.name: Madagascar
MDG.description: Madagascar, après la crise politique de 2009-2013, tente de stabiliser son économie sous Rajaonarimampianina, restant très dépendant de l'aide internationale.
MDG.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Madagascar]
- Political landscape: President Hery Rajaonarimampianina restoring democratic governance after 2009 crisis; weak institutions, political elite factionalism.
- Key challenges: Extreme poverty (~75% below poverty line), deforestation threatening unique biodiversity, cyclone vulnerability, food insecurity, plague outbreaks.
- Economic situation: One of world's poorest countries despite natural resources; ~3.1% growth; vanilla and clove exports; mining (nickel, cobalt) growing; tourism potential unrealized.

MWI.name: Malawi
MWI.description: Le Malawi est l'un des pays les plus pauvres au monde, très dépendant de l'aide étrangère et du tabac, enclavé entre le Mozambique, la Tanzanie et la Zambie.
MWI.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Malawi]
- Political landscape: President Peter Mutharika (DPP) governing; 'Cashgate' corruption scandal (theft of $32M in government funds) damaging donor relations.
- Key challenges: Extreme poverty, HIV/AIDS prevalence (~10%), chronic food insecurity, Cashgate fallout reducing donor aid, tobacco dependence, climate vulnerability (floods and droughts).
- Economic situation: One of world's poorest countries (GDP per capita ~$350); tobacco main export; heavily aid-dependent; growth ~2.9%; kwacha depreciation driving inflation.

NAM.name: Namibia
NAM.description: La Namibie est l'une des démocraties les plus stables d'Afrique australe, riche en ressources naturelles mais confrontée aux inégalités héritées de l'apartheid.
NAM.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Namibia]
- Political landscape: President Hage Geingob (SWAPO) took office March 2015; SWAPO dominant since independence 1990; relatively good governance by regional standards.
- Key challenges: Extreme inequality (one of world's highest Gini coefficients), land reform pressures (colonial-era imbalances), drought, unemployment (~28%), HIV/AIDS.
- Economic situation: Upper-middle-income country; mining (diamonds, uranium) key sector; tourism growing; growth ~5%; drought threatening agriculture.

BWA.name: Botswana
BWA.description: Le Botswana est souvent cité comme modèle de gouvernance en Afrique, transformant ses revenus diamantifères en développement humain, malgré une forte dépendance économique vis-à-vis de l'Afrique du Sud.
BWA.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Botswana]
- Political landscape: President Ian Khama (BDP); one of Africa's most stable democracies; BDP in power since independence 1966; good governance reputation.
- Key challenges: HIV/AIDS prevalence among world's highest (~22%), economic dependence on diamonds, income inequality, unemployment especially among youth, drought.
- Economic situation: Upper-middle-income country, diamond exports (70-80% of export earnings), growth slowing to ~3% as diamond demand weakens; Debswana partnership with De Beers.

LSO.name: Lesotho
LSO.description: Enclave totalement entourée par l'Afrique du Sud dont il est économiquement dépendant, le Lesotho exporte son eau et ses travailleurs vers son grand voisin.
LSO.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Lesotho]
- Political landscape: PM Pakalitha Mosisili in power after 2015 snap elections; political instability and military factionalism; SADC mediating political crisis.
- Key challenges: HIV/AIDS prevalence among world's highest (~23%), poverty, political instability, complete economic dependence on South Africa, unemployment.
- Economic situation: Tiny landlocked economy entirely surrounded by South Africa; diamond mining, garment exports, and water exports (Lesotho Highlands Water Project); heavily remittance-dependent.

SWZ.name: Eswatini
SWZ.description: Dernière monarchie absolue d'Afrique subsaharienne, l'Eswatini (ex-Swaziland) est gouvernée par Mswati III et dépend fortement de l'Afrique du Sud.
SWZ.nation_brief:
[NATION BRIEF — Eswatini (Swaziland)]
- Political landscape: King Mswati III ruling as Africa's last absolute monarch; political parties banned; traditional governance system; youth frustration growing.
- Key challenges: World's highest HIV/AIDS prevalence (~27%), poverty (~63%), absolute monarchy limiting freedoms, youth unemployment, dependence on South Africa.
- Economic situation: Small economy tied to South Africa (Southern African Customs Union revenues); sugar and soft drink concentrate exports; textile industry; growth ~1.7%.
